VSLBLD.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” the VSL KIDS base build definition + env-check binding (packaging seam).
VSLBLD.description:
doc: @exrun live

Defines the VSL layer's KIDS base build and the build-time facts a consumer
needs. The build itself is the drift-gated artifact kids/vsl.build.json ->
dist/kids/VSL.kids (gated by `make check-kids`): all the VSL* routines + the
VPNG GREETING #8989.51 PARAMETER DEFINITION + a Required Build on the m-stdlib
base (MSL*0.1*1, so STD* is reused from one shared install, never copied in)
+ the VSLENV environment-check routine. VSLBLD binds ONLY the KIDS/Kernel
programmer API (ICR #10141, XPDUTL); the actual install / verify / back-out
is performed by v-pkg over the driver â€” VSLBLD does NOT duplicate v-pkg's
install mechanics (the in-`v` no-duplication rule; architecture Â§7.2).

Public API:
  $$manifest^VSLBLD(out)     â€” fill out() with the base routines + Required Build + patch identity
  $$envCheck^VSLBLD(facts)   â€” the environment facts (engine/version/Kernel/TLS) via VSLENV (v->v)
  $$requireBase^VSLBLD(build) â€” 1 iff KIDS build `build` is installed (the R6 version-skew check)
  $$lastError^VSLBLD()       â€” last error detail, else ""

*** ERROR CONTRACT â€” loud on a malformed call, never on a normal negative ***
A malformed call (an empty build name) maps to a clean ,U-VSL-BLD-ARG, $ECODE
with the detail in ^TMP($job,"vslbld","err") for $$lastError. A base that is
simply NOT installed is a normal 0 from $$requireBase â€” NOT an error (kickoff
decision 4, the VSLSEC DENY-is-not-an-error posture).

manifest.synopsis: Fill out() with the VSL base's routines, its Required Build and patch identity; return the routine count.

returns.doc: the number of routines the VSL base ships

examples: do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$manifest^VSLBLD(.out)'<5,"the base ships at least the five M1-M4 VSL* modules")
set n=$$manifest^VSLBLD(.out) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$get(out("requiredBuild"))="MSL*0.1*1","manifest declares the Required Build on the m-stdlib base")
set n=$$manifest^VSLBLD(.out) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$get(out("patch"))="VSL*1.0*3","manifest declares the patch identity VSL*1.0*3")

envCheck.synopsis: The environment facts (engine/version/Kernel/TLS) via the self-contained VSLENV (v->v).

returns.doc: 1 on success

examples: do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$envCheck^VSLBLD(.facts)=1,"$$envCheck succeeds on a live VistA")
set ok=$$envCheck^VSLBLD(.facts) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$get(facts("engine"))'="","env-check reports the engine type")

requireBase.synopsis: 1 iff KIDS build `build` is installed on this system (the R6 version-skew check).

returns.doc: 1 iff installed; 0 (a normal not-installed result) otherwise

raises.doc: the call is malformed (an empty build name)

examples: do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$requireBase^VSLBLD("ZZNOSUCH*9.9*9")=0,"an absent base build is a normal 0 (a not-installed result, not a loud failure)")
do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set x=$$requireBase^VSLBLD("""")","U-VSL-BLD-ARG","$$requireBase with no build name raises U-VSL-BLD-...")

lastError.synopsis: The last VSLBLD error message (the composed malformed-call detail).

returns.doc: ^TMP($job,"vslbld","err"), or "" if none

examples: do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set x=$$requireBase^VSLBLD("""")","U-VSL-BLD-ARG","arming the error state") do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$lastError^VSLBLD()'="","lastError carries the malformed-call detail after a rejected call")

VSLCFG.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” VistA configuration adapter over XPAR (Parameter Tools).
VSLCFG.description:
doc: @exrun live
doc: @exsafe transactional

Binds the MSL config-read seam ($$get^STDENV) to VistA's XPAR parameter
store at the SYS (system) entity â€” the faithful analog of STDENV's flat
key->value config read. The adapter contains ONLY the VistA binding; no
parsing or formatting (that stays in STD*, called up; m/v waterline).

Public extrinsics:
  $$get^VSLCFG(key,default)   â€” read a SYS-level parameter value, else default
  $$set^VSLCFG(key,value)     â€” set a parameter value at the SYS entity

XPAR is a Supported API (Kernel Toolkit, ICR #2263).

get.synopsis: Read parameter `key` at the SYS entity; return `default` when unset.

params.name: default
params.doc: value returned when the parameter is unset

returns.doc: the SYS-level value, or `default` when unset

examples: do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$get^VSLCFG("ZZVSLCFGNOSUCH","fallback"),"fallback","get: unset parameter returns the default")

new k,i,r,d set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)="@",U="^",DT=$$DT^XLFDT,k="",d=0 for  set k=$order(^XTV(8989.51,"B",k)) quit:k=""!d  set i=+$order(^XTV(8989.51,"B",k,0)) if i,$extract($get(^XTV(8989.51,i,6)))="F",$$GET^XPAR("SYS",k,1)="" do EN^XPAR("SYS",k,1,"ZZP",.r) set r=$$GET^XPAR("SYS",k,1) do EN^XPAR("SYS",k,1,"@") if r="ZZP" do set^VSLCFG(k,"hi") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$get^VSLCFG(k,"MISS"),"hi","get: $$set then $$get round-trips a SYS value") do EN^XPAR("SYS",k,1,"@") set d=1

set.synopsis: Set parameter `key` to `value` at the SYS entity.

params.name: value
params.doc: value to store at the SYS level

returns.doc: side-effecting; no return value

examples: new k,i,r,d set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)="@",U="^",DT=$$DT^XLFDT,k="",d=0 for  set k=$order(^XTV(8989.51,"B",k)) quit:k=""!d  set i=+$order(^XTV(8989.51,"B",k,0)) if i,$extract($get(^XTV(8989.51,i,6)))="F",$$GET^XPAR("SYS",k,1)="" do EN^XPAR("SYS",k,1,"ZZP",.r) set r=$$GET^XPAR("SYS",k,1) do EN^XPAR("SYS",k,1,"@") if r="ZZP" do set^VSLCFG(k,"hi") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$get^VSLCFG(k,"MISS"),"hi","set: stores a SYS value that $$get reads back") do EN^XPAR("SYS",k,1,"@") set d=1

VSLENV.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” the VSL KIDS environment-check routine (the XPDENV hook).
VSLENV.description:
doc: @exrun live

The single, SELF-CONTAINED environment-check routine named by the VSL KIDS
base build (kids/vsl.build.json "envCheck"). KIDS loads ONLY this routine on
the target at check time and runs it TWICE â€” once at Load a Distribution and
again at Install (the key variable XPDENV signals the phase) â€” so it must not
call any other VSL*/STD* routine from the build (none are loaded yet); it
uses only intrinsics + RESIDENT Kernel APIs (architecture Â§7.2, KIDS DG).

It fails fast on a genuine showstopper (Kernel absent -> XPDQUIT) and reports
the engine type/version, Kernel patch level and TLS-config presence â€” the
facts a VWEB-class consumer Requires and extends (engine, TLS, Kernel level,
IRIS-for-Health minimum). The programmatic $$check entry returns those facts
without touching KIDS state, so VSLBLD/tests can read them off-install.

Public API:
  VSLENV            â€” the KIDS env-check entry (run by KIDS; honors XPDENV/XPDQUIT)
  $$check^VSLENV(facts) â€” fill facts(engine,version,kernel,tls); always returns 1

abort.synopsis: (private) a genuine showstopper â€” Kernel (XU) is not present; abort the install.
abort.illustrative: only meaningful inside a live KIDS install â€” MES^XPDUTL needs the KIDS message buffer + it sets the KIDS XPDQUIT install-control flag

check.synopsis: Fill facts(engine,version,kernel,tls) from intrinsics + resident Kernel; return 1.

returns.doc: always 1 (faultable reads are isolated + trapped)

examples: set x=$$check^VSLENV(.facts) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,x,1,"check returns 1")
set x=$$check^VSLENV(.facts) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,facts("engine")'="","check fills a non-empty engine fact")
set x=$$check^VSLENV(.facts) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,facts("version"),$zversion,"check reports the running engine version")

kernelVer.synopsis: (private) the Kernel (#9.4 XU) current version, "" if unavailable.
examples: do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$kernelVer^VSLENV()'="","kernelVer is non-empty on a Kernel-equipped VistA")

tlsConfig.synopsis: (private) the DEFAULT TLS SERVER CONFIG Kernel System Parameter (presence), "" if unset.
examples: do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$tlsConfig^VSLENV(),$$GET^XPAR("SYS","DEFAULT TLS SERVER CONFIG",1),"tlsConfig reads the DEFAULT TLS SERVER CONFIG parameter")

VSLFS.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” VistA FileMan storage adapter (FileMan DBS record store).
VSLFS.description:
doc: @exrun live
doc: @exsafe transactional
m-lint: disable-file=M-MOD-024
M-MOD-024 false positives: the analyser reads the FileMan DBS I/O arrays
(FDA / IEN / ERR, written by the called DBS routine by-reference) as
locals-before-def; they are the documented GETS/UPDATE/FILE convention.
Same suppression as VSLIO/STDNET.

Binds the MSL storage seam (STDKV, S1) to VistA's FileMan Database Server
(DBS) API: a record store addressed by (file, iens, field). It exposes the
same four-verb signature as STDKV â€” $$set/$$get/$$exists/$$kill â€” backed by
FileMan DBS calls, never direct global access (architecture Â§3.2). The
adapter contains ONLY the VistA binding; any non-FileMan logic stays in the
MSL seam, called up (m/v waterline Â§9 no-duplication).

Public API (the handle is a FileMan IENS; values are field values):
  $$set^VSLFS(file,iens,field,value) â€” file a field (UPDATE^DIE); add a
                                        record with iens "+1," -> resolved IENS
  $$get^VSLFS(file,iens,field,default)â€” read a field ($$GET1^DIQ), else default
  $$exists^VSLFS(file,iens)           â€” 1 iff the record exists
  $$kill^VSLFS(file,iens)             â€” delete the record (FILE^DIE, .01="@")
  $$lastError^VSLFS()                 â€” last FileMan DIERR detail, else ""

*** ERROR CONTRACT â€” loud, never a silent wrong value ***
A FileMan DIERR on a write maps to a clean ,U-VSL-FS-DIERR, $ECODE, with the
DIERR text composed into ^TMP($job,"vslfs","err") for $$lastError. Reads of
an absent record/field are NOT errors â€” $$get returns the default and
$$exists returns 0 (the STDKV "absent -> default" semantics). Every DBS call
passes an explicit MSG_ROOT ("ERR") so errors land in this adapter's own
array, never the shared ^TMP("DIERR",$J).

ICR note: the FileMan DBS API is the public DBS programmer API (FileMan
Developer's Guide, custodian DI). The DBIA/ICR *number* is notional â€” a
manually-curated FORUM list, not enforced programmatically â€” so each call is
tagged `@icr DBS` (the notional marker), with a real @status/@custodian/
@source. See docs/memory notional-dbia-not-a-blocker + plan Â§5.4.

set.synopsis: File `value` into (file,iens,field); return the resolved IENS, else raise.

returns.doc: the resolved IENS on success (the new IENS for an add)

examples: do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)=""@"",U=""^"",DT=$$DT^XLFDT set x=$$set^VSLFS(99999999,""+1,"","".01"",""ZZ"")","U-VSL-FS","set: a FileMan DIERR raises U-VSL-FS-DIERR")
set.illustrative: a successful add files a real FileMan record; demonstrating it needs a throwaway test DD (#999000 ZZVSLFS) created+deleted, not a safe read-only one-liner â€” see tests/VSLFSTST.m tCreateGetRoundtrip

params.name: default
params.doc: value returned when the field/record is unset

returns.doc: the external field value, or `default`

examples: set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)="@",U="^",DT=$$DT^XLFDT do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$get^VSLFS(200,"1,",".01","")'="","get: #200 IEN 1 (.01) reads a non-empty name")
set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)="@",U="^",DT=$$DT^XLFDT do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$get^VSLFS(200,"999999999,",".01","MISS"),"MISS","get: an absent record returns the default")

exists.synopsis: Return 1 iff record (file,iens) exists (its .01 reads without a DIERR).

returns.doc: 1 iff the record exists; 0 otherwise

examples: set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)="@",U="^",DT=$$DT^XLFDT do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$exists^VSLFS(200,"1,"),1,"exists: #200 IEN 1 (postmaster) exists")
set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)="@",U="^",DT=$$DT^XLFDT do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$exists^VSLFS(200,"999999999,"),0,"exists: an absent record returns 0")

kill.synopsis: Delete record (file,iens) via an FDA .01="@" through FILE^DIE; return 1.

params.name: iens
params.doc: IENS of the record to delete

returns.doc: 1 (idempotent â€” a DIERR is recorded, not raised)

kill.illustrative: deletes a real FileMan record (a persistent mutation); demonstrating it safely needs a throwaway record created+deleted in a test DD (#999000 ZZVSLFS), not a read-only one-liner â€” see tests/VSLFSTST.m tExistsThenKill

lastError.synopsis: The last VSLFS error message (the composed FileMan DIERR detail).

returns.doc: ^TMP($job,"vslfs","err"), or "" if none

examples: new prior,r set prior=$get(^TMP($job,"vslfs","err")),^TMP($job,"vslfs","err")="set: FileMan DIERR" set r=$$lastError^VSLFS() set ^TMP($job,"vslfs","err")=prior do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,r,"set: FileMan DIERR","lastError: returns the composed FileMan DIERR detail")

VSLHL7TAP.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” HL7 store-tail adapter (decoupled, zero in-line).
VSLHL7TAP.description:
doc: @exrun bare

Phase 3 / M2 of the RPC+HL7 -> S3 traffic tap (spec Â§4, D-3). The HL7 half of
the tap. Unlike VSLRPCTAP (an in-line tee BESIDE the ephemeral RPC runner),
the HL7 traffic is ALREADY persisted by the HL7 package, so VSLHL7TAP is a
passive STORE-TAILER: a separate flush process (VSLTASK, like VSLS3 $$drain)
reads new entries forward from a saved cursor and tees each verbatim message
into the same VSLTAP ring. Non-interference is STRUCTURAL â€” it only READS the
stores the HL7 send/receive path already wrote, adding nothing to that path.

*** G-HL7HOOK resolved by a LIVE probe of vehu (YDB-VistA) 2026-06-20 + the
#772/#778/#777 DD: VistA keeps TWO parallel HL7 message stores and both can
carry live traffic, so the tail covers both:

  legacy HL7 (#772, ^HL(772,) â€” package-managed, NOT a FileMan WP field:
    ^HL(772,IEN,0)            = fmDateTime^^^DIR^^MSGID^^IEN^STATUS^...
    ^HL(772,IEN,"IN",0)       = WP header ^^lastseq^count^date^
    ^HL(772,IEN,"IN",seq,0)   = one verbatim HL7 segment per node (seq 1..N)
  HLO (#778 ^HLB / #777 ^HLA):
    ^HLB(IEN,0)               = MSGID^bodyIEN^^DIR^LINK   (.02 -> #777)
    ^HLB(IEN,1) / ^HLB(IEN,2) = MSH components 1-6 / 7-end (rebuilt header)
    ^HLA(bodyIEN,1,seq,0)     = one verbatim body segment (MSH excluded)

The reliable tail cursor is the LAST PRESENT numeric IEN ($ORDER), NOT the
file 0-node's 3rd piece â€” the probe found ^HL(772,0) piece-3 stale (4589) while
the live entries keyed at ~2.23M; HLO assigns IENs from ^HLC, not a 0-node +1.
Non-numeric top-level subscripts (the B/C/AF/AI cross-references) are skipped.

Layer: v. Consumes the VSLTAP core (v->v, consumer-gated + fault-fenced via
$$tee^VSLTAP); the engine seam stays in VSLTAP. Cursors ride in ^VSLTAP.

Public API:
  do tail()              â€” tail BOTH stores once (gated; ship every new message)
  do tailLegacy()        â€” tail #772 forward from its cursor
  do tailHLO()           â€” tail #778/#777 forward from its cursor
  $$readLegacy(ien)      â€” verbatim reassembly of a #772 entry ("" if purged)
  $$readHLO(ien)         â€” verbatim reassembly of a #778/#777 entry
  $$cursor(store)        â€” the persisted high-water IEN ("772" | "778")
  do setCursor(store,ien) / resetCursors()   â€” cursor controls

tail.synopsis: Tail both HL7 stores once: ship every newly-persisted message into the ring.

returns.doc: consumer-gated at the top (no consumer -> no tail, cursors

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP"),^HL(772),^HLB,^HLA  do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1)  set ^HL(772,10,0)="3170627.01^^^O^^L1^^10^D",^HL(772,10,"IN",1,0)="MSH|leg",^HL(772,10,"IN",0)="^^1^1^3170627^"  set ^HLB(3,0)="H1^200^^O^link",^HLB(3,1)="MSH|^~\&|",^HLB(3,2)="|ADT",^HLA(200,1,1,0)="EVN|hlo",^HLA(200,1,0)="^^1^1^3170627^"  do tail^VSLHL7TAP()  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$size^VSLTAP(),2,"one tail pass drains both the legacy and the HLO store")

kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP"),^HL(772)  do arm^VSLTAP()  set ^HL(772,10,0)="3170627^^^O^^L1^^10^D",^HL(772,10,"IN",1,0)="MSH|x",^HL(772,10,"IN",0)="^^1^1^3170627^"  do tail^VSLHL7TAP()  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$size^VSLTAP(),0,"no consumer -> zero capture, cursor frozen for catch-up on re-arm")
tail.description: frozen so a re-arm catches up); each store tail is independently fenced.

tailLegacy.synopsis: Tail #772 forward from its cursor, teeing each new verbatim message.
examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP"),^HL(772)  do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1)  set ^HL(772,2230625,0)="3170627.01^^^O^^ID1^^2230625^D",^HL(772,2230625,"IN",1,0)="MSH|^~\&|A",^HL(772,2230625,"IN",0)="^^1^1^3170627^"  set ^HL(772,2230648,0)="3170627.01^^^I^^ID2^^2230648^D",^HL(772,2230648,"IN",1,0)="MSH|^~\&|B",^HL(772,2230648,"IN",0)="^^1^1^3170627^"  do tailLegacy^VSLHL7TAP()  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$cursor^VSLHL7TAP("772"),2230648,"the #772 cursor advances to the last IEN after tailing both entries")

tailHLO.synopsis: Tail #778/#777 forward from its cursor, teeing each new verbatim message.
examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP"),^HLB,^HLA  do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1)  set ^HLB(7,0)="MID^400^^O^link",^HLB(7,1)="MSH|^~\&|X|",^HLB(7,2)="Y||ADT^A01|MID|P|2.4",^HLA(400,1,1,0)="EVN|A01",^HLA(400,1,0)="^^1^1^3170627^"  do tailHLO^VSLHL7TAP()  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$cursor^VSLHL7TAP("778"),7,"the #778 cursor advances after tailing the HLO entry")

readLegacy.synopsis: Reassemble the verbatim CR-delimited message for #772 entry `ien`.

returns.doc: segments ^HL(772,ien,"IN",seq,0) joined by $C(13),

examples: set ^HL(772,100,"IN",1,0)="MSH|^~\&|ROR SITE",^HL(772,100,"IN",2,0)="PID|1||0",^HL(772,100,"IN",3,0)="CSR|VA HEPC"  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$readLegacy^VSLHL7TAP(100),"MSH|^~\&|ROR SITE"_$char(13)_"PID|1||0"_$char(13)_"CSR|VA HEPC","the three #772 'IN' segments rejoin byte-verbatim, CR-delimited")
set ^HL(772,200,0)="3170717.234501^^^^^5002230648"  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$readLegacy^VSLHL7TAP(200),"","a purged #772 entry (header only, no 'IN' body) returns the empty string")
readLegacy.description: in seq order; "" when the body was purged (header-only entry).

readHLO.synopsis: Reassemble the verbatim message for HLO #778 entry `ien` (MSH + body).

returns.doc: the MSH (^HLB(ien,1)_^HLB(ien,2)) then the #777 body

examples: set ^HLB(50,0)="MSGID123^900^^O^link",^HLB(50,1)="MSH|^~\&|APP|FAC|",^HLB(50,2)="DEST|FAC2",^HLA(900,1,1,0)="EVN|A01",^HLA(900,1,2,0)="PID|1||12345"  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$readHLO^VSLHL7TAP(50),"MSH|^~\&|APP|FAC|DEST|FAC2"_$char(13)_"EVN|A01"_$char(13)_"PID|1||12345","MSH(nodes 1+2) prepended to the #777 body segments, CR-delimited, byte-verbatim")

cursor.synopsis: The persisted high-water IEN for a store ("772" | "778"); 0 if unset.

returns.doc: the last IEN tailed

examples: kill ^VSLTAP("hl7cur","772")  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$cursor^VSLHL7TAP("772"),0,"an unset cursor reads as 0")
set ^VSLTAP("hl7cur","778")=4242  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$cursor^VSLHL7TAP("778"),4242,"cursor returns the persisted high-water IEN")  kill ^VSLTAP("hl7cur","778")

setCursor.synopsis: Persist the high-water IEN for a store.
examples: do setCursor^VSLHL7TAP("772",99)  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$cursor^VSLHL7TAP("772"),99,"setCursor persists the high-water IEN, read back by $$cursor")  kill ^VSLTAP("hl7cur","772")

resetCursors.synopsis: Clear both cursors (re-tail from the beginning of each store).
examples: set ^VSLTAP("hl7cur","772")=5,^VSLTAP("hl7cur","778")=9  do resetCursors^VSLHL7TAP()  do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$cursor^VSLHL7TAP("772")+$$cursor^VSLHL7TAP("778"),0,"resetCursors clears both cursors back to 0")

VSLIO.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” VistA TCP transport adapter over the Kernel device handler.
VSLIO.description:
doc: @exrun live
m-lint: disable-file=M-MOD-024
M-MOD-024 false positives: the analyser reads the Kernel device-handler
input variables (IPADDRESS/SOCKET/TIMEOUT/IO/POP) and the device USE/READ
targets as locals-before-def; they are the documented ^%ZISTCP I/O
convention. Same suppression as STDJSON/STDHTTP/STDNET.

Binds the MSL socket seam (STDNET, S4) to VistA's Kernel device handler:
outbound TCP via CALL^%ZISTCP / CLOSE^%ZISTCP (ICR #2118, Supported). It
exposes the CLIENT subset of STDNET's signature (connect/read/write/close)
â€” VistA has NO Supported Kernel listen/accept (server) API (Kernel Device
Handler DG; inbound is the listener-process/JOB pattern), so the SERVER side
of a connection stays in the portable STDNET seam, never duplicated here.
The adapter contains ONLY the VistA binding; framing/buffering stays in
STD* and is called up (m/v waterline).

Public API (raw bytes; the handle is the opened device, $$connect's return):
  $$connect^VSLIO(host,port,timeout)   â€” CALL^%ZISTCP outbound -> handle or 0
  $$read^VSLIO(id,maxlen,timeout,.buf) â€” raw read up to maxlen bytes -> count
  $$write^VSLIO(id,buf)                â€” raw write -> 1/0
  $$close^VSLIO(id)                    â€” CLOSE^%ZISTCP -> 1
  $$lastError^VSLIO()                  â€” last error message, else ""

*** SECURITY / TLS GAP â€” same posture as STDNET (loud, never silent) ***
This adapter opens RAW PLAINTEXT TCP. TLS (Kernel $$INIT^XUTLS, ICR #7616,
using a named config defaulting to the DEFAULT TLS SERVER CONFIG parameter)
is NOT wired: it requires engine TLS infrastructure absent on the test
engines (a cert + Kernel patch XU*8.0*787 / an IRIS Security.SSLConfigs
entry; IRIS-only per the gold corpus). So $$tlsAvailable^VSLIO()=0 and
$$connectTls^VSLIO RAISES ,U-VSLIO-NOTLS, (with $$tlsHelp/$$lastError
carrying remediation) rather than fall back to plaintext. This is a GATING
cleanup before the MSL/VSL stack is complete â€” see STDNET's TLS gap
(m-stdlib docs/tracking/discoveries.md, 2026-06-16) and VSLIO tier-3.

The last error message is stashed at ^TMP($job,"vslio","err") for $$lastError.
Errors set $ECODE to one of:
  ,U-VSLIO-NOTLS,    TLS requested but not wired (see $$tlsHelp)

connect.synopsis: Open an outbound TCP connection; return the device handle, else 0.

returns.doc: the opened device (handle) on POP=0, else 0

examples: do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$connect^VSLIO("127.0.0.1",65000,2)=0,"connect to a closed port returns 0 (POP positive)")
connect.description:
@icr 2118 @call CALL^%ZISTCP @status Supported @custodian XU @source XU/krn_8_0_dg_device_handler_ug#callzistcp-make-tcpip-connection-remote-system
WARNING: PLAINTEXT â€” no TLS (see $$tlsAvailable / $$connectTls; known gap).

read.synopsis: Raw-read up to maxlen bytes from a handle.

returns.doc: bytes read (0 on timeout/EOF)

read.illustrative: needs a live connected handle ($$connect + a peer writing bytes); see tests/VSLIOTST.m tLoopbackEcho (STDNET loopback server).

returns.doc: 1 on success

write.illustrative: needs a live connected handle from $$connect to USE/WRITE; see tests/VSLIOTST.m tLoopbackEcho.

close.synopsis: Close an outbound connection opened by $$connect.

returns.doc: 1 (idempotent)

close.illustrative: needs a live handle from $$connect to CLOSE^%ZISTCP; see tests/VSLIOTST.m tLoopbackEcho.

lastError.synopsis: The last VSLIO error message (e.g. the TLS-gap remediation).

returns.doc: ^TMP($job,"vslio","err"), or "" if none

examples: new had,save set had=$data(^TMP($job,"vslio","err")),save=$get(^TMP($job,"vslio","err")),^TMP($job,"vslio","err")="connectTls: x" do contains^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$lastError^VSLIO(),"connectTls","lastError returns the stashed message") if had set ^TMP($job,"vslio","err")=save quit:had  kill ^TMP($job,"vslio","err")

tlsAvailable.synopsis: 0 â€” VSLIO has no wired TLS (engine TLS infra + XU*8.0*787 absent).

returns.doc: always 0 today: raw plaintext only (a known, tracked gap)

examples: write $$tlsAvailable^VSLIO()  ; 0
tlsAvailable.description: Check before any secure use; $$tlsHelp has remediation.

tlsHelp.synopsis: Human-readable remediation for the TLS gap (diagnostics/logs).

returns.doc: multi-line: why there is no TLS + how to remedy

examples: do contains^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$tlsHelp^VSLIO(),"NOTLS","tlsHelp carries the remediation message")

connectTls.synopsis: UNIMPLEMENTED â€” raises, never opens plaintext.

params.name: config
params.doc: named TLS config (ignored â€” not implemented)

returns.doc: never returns a handle; always raises

examples: do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set x=$$connectTls^VSLIO(""h"",1,1,""cfg"")","U-VSLIO-NOTLS","connectTls raises U-VSLIO-NOTLS")

VSLLOG.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” VistA FileMan audit-sink adapter (the S3 audit seam).
VSLLOG.description:
doc: @exrun live
doc: @exsafe transactional

Binds the observability sink to a VistA FileMan audit file. VSLLOG is the
first v->v composition: it writes audit records by REUSING VSLFS (the
FileMan DBS record writer) rather than re-binding UPDATE^DIE/$$GET1^DIQ
itself â€” the in-`v` analog of the waterline no-duplication rule (a `v` tool
consumes a lower `v` capability; only `v->m`/leaked-VistA-symbols are
forbidden, never a VSL*->VSL* call). VSLLOG adds ONLY the log-record ->
FileMan-field mapping: it composes a timestamped audit line (the timestamp
from $$now^STDDATE(), portable, called up â€” v->m) and files it as the
record's .01 via $$set^VSLFS.

Public API (the handle is the FileMan IENS VSLFS returns):
  $$write^VSLLOG(file,event,detail) â€” file one audit record -> resolved IENS
  $$read^VSLLOG(file,iens)          â€” read an audit line back, else ""
  $$lastError^VSLLOG()              â€” last error detail, else ""

*** ERROR CONTRACT â€” loud, never a silent lost record ***
A FileMan write failure surfaces from VSLFS as ,U-VSL-FS-DIERR,; VSLLOG
catches it and re-raises a clean ,U-VSL-LOG-WRITE, $ECODE, carrying the
underlying VSLFS detail in ^TMP($job,"vsllog","err") for $$lastError. The
"audit log must never silently drop a record" goal (Â§6.2): a sink failure is
loud, not swallowed. Reads of an absent record return "" (as VSLFS reads do).

No @icr declarations here: VSLLOG makes NO direct L4 call â€” every FileMan
DBS call is inside VSLFS (declared there), and $$now^STDDATE is an `m`-layer
(STD*) call up, not an L4 reference. The v->v + v->m composition is correct
by construction and invisible to the ICR/no-direct-global gate.

write.synopsis: File one audit record into `file`; return the resolved IENS, else raise.

returns.doc: the resolved IENS of the new audit record

examples: set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)="@",U="^",DT=$$DT^XLFDT,ie=$$write^VSLLOG(8989.51,"ZZVSLLOGEX","X") do contains^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$read^VSLLOG(8989.51,ie),"ZZVSLLOGEX","write then read-back contains the event") set zzok=$$kill^VSLFS(8989.51,ie)
set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)="@",U="^",DT=$$DT^XLFDT do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set x=$$write^VSLLOG(99999999,""ZZ"",""X"")","U-VSL-LOG-WRITE","writing into a bogus file raises U-VSL-LOG-WRITE")

read.synopsis: Read the audit line stored at (file,iens) .01, else "".

returns.doc: the stored audit line, or "" if absent

examples: set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)="@",U="^",DT=$$DT^XLFDT do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$read^VSLLOG(8989.51,"9999999,"),"","read of an absent record returns empty string")

lastError.synopsis: The last VSLLOG error message (the composed FileMan detail).

returns.doc: ^TMP($job,"vsllog","err"), or "" if none

examples: set DUZ=1,DUZ(0)="@",U="^",DT=$$DT^XLFDT do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set x=$$write^VSLLOG(99999999,""ZZ"",""X"")","U-VSL-LOG-WRITE","seed a failure") do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$lastError^VSLLOG()'="","lastError carries the FileMan detail after a failed write")

VSLRPCTAP.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” RPC tap adapter at the VSLRPC chokepoint (the fenced tee).
VSLRPCTAP.description:
doc: @exrun bare

The thin RPC adapter over the VSLTAP core (spec Â§4.1.3, Â§6.1). VSLRPC is the
ephemeral RPC runner (proposed, unbuilt) â€” so this session wires the seam,
not a live runner: the real VSLRPC will call `do capture^VSLRPCTAP(.msg)` as
a side-call BESIDE `D TAG^ROUTINE`, exactly the way the tests inject it. The
tee's whole contract is to be invisible to the RPC path:

  - It is fire-and-forget: its return is ignored, its arg is scratch (read,
    never written), and it touches no symbol the caller's result / $ECODE /
    $T / IO depends on.
  - The fault fence lives in $$tee^VSLTAP (flag-based $ETRAP, dual-engine);
    ANY fault inside the tap is swallowed there and self-disables capture.
  - It NEWs $TEST so the caller's $T survives the side-call untouched.

The append itself is one fast ^XTMP SET into the rolling ring (no LOCK, no
serialize, no socket, no I/O, no block) â€” the irreducible minimum a tap on an
ephemeral protocol can be (HL7, already persisted in #772, needs even less).

Layer: v. Consumes the VSLTAP core (v->v); the engine seam stays in VSLTAP.

Public API:
  do capture^VSLRPCTAP(.rec)   â€” fenced fire-and-forget tee of one RPC record (cache layout v2)
  $$callId^VSLRPCTAP(station,ctr) â€” build a station-$J-ctr correlation id (FU-14)

The record `rec` is a by-ref descriptor (schema-lock Â§2/Â§3): rec("dir")="req"|"resp",
rec("rpc"), rec("payload") (verbatim params for a req / scalar result for a resp),
rec("gref") (closed-root global ref to MERGE for a GLOBAL ARRAY result, FU-17),
rec("result_kind"), rec("call_id") (correlates the req with its resp; the wrap bumps a
$J-scoped local counter once per RPC and shares it across both side-calls), and the
FU-18 context rec("duz")/rec("job")/rec("client")/rec("station")/rec("denied"). The tap
reads it; it never writes it (scratch contract).

capture.synopsis: Fenced fire-and-forget tee of one RPC record (cache layout v2) into the rolling ring.

params.name: rec
params.doc: by-ref record descriptor (read-only; the tap never mutates it)

returns.doc: fire-and-forget â€” the result/$ECODE/$T/naked-ref of the RPC worker are untouched

examples: new rec do off^VSLTAP(),arm^VSLTAP() kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") set rec("dir")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-1",rec("rpc")="ORWU DT",rec("payload")="DUZ=10^NOW" do capture^VSLRPCTAP(.rec) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$size^VSLTAP(),1,"capture tees one record into the always-on ring") do off^VSLTAP() kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP")
capture.description:
This is the boundary the FU-5 wrap calls, so the caller-state fence lives HERE:
$TEST and the naked indicator ($REFERENCE on YDB / $ZREFERENCE on IRIS) are
snapshotted at entry â€” before any global reference â€” and re-established on EVERY
exit. naked-reference save/restore (FU-4, R-NAKED) is the keystone: the tap's ^XTMP
SETs mutate the caller's naked indicator, so without the restore the caller's next
`^(sub)` would silently hit ^XTMP. The SVN differs by engine (neither compiles the
other's token), so $$nakedRef reads it dual-engine; the restore is one benign FULL
reference `s zz=$d(@nref)` â€” it re-points the indicator without reading the value.
The restore runs in the FINALLY path (after a DO-framed worker) so it fires on
success, gating, AND a swallowed fault â€” and is the LAST global op before return.
The `nref'=""` guard handles a job-start empty indicator (AC-2). $TEST is restored
by hand (NOT `new $test` â€” that aborts on IRIS); `if t` reproduces 0/1 exactly.

callId.synopsis: Build a correlation call_id = station "-" $J "-" ctr (schema-lock Â§2).

params.name: ctr
params.doc: a $J-scoped counter the wrap bumps ONCE per RPC invocation

returns.doc: the call_id shared by that RPC's req + resp records

examples: do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$callId^VSLRPCTAP("500",7),"500-"_$job_"-7","callId builds station-$J-counter")
callId.description:
A process-local counter (not a global hot node, spec Â§4.1.3) keeps event_id
(call_id ":" direction) deterministic without cross-process contention.

work.synopsis: (private) the global-touching side, DO-framed so a fault can never escape the boundary.

params.name: rec
params.doc: by-ref record descriptor (the caller's array is untouched)

examples: new rec do off^VSLTAP(),arm^VSLTAP() kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") set rec("dir")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-1",rec("rpc")="X",rec("payload")="P" do work^VSLRPCTAP(.rec) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$size^VSLTAP(),1,"work appends the record (the global-touching side)") do off^VSLTAP() kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP")
work.description:
$$teeRec^VSLTAP is already self-fenced (it swallows + self-disables); this frame's
flag-based $ETRAP is the backstop so that even a residual escape returns here
via an arg-less QUIT, letting capture's finally restore the naked indicator.
MUST stay DO-invoked (never `$$`): the trap's arg-less QUIT raises M17
NOTEXTRINSIC in an extrinsic frame (same gotcha as append/write1).

nakedRef.synopsis: (private) the caller's last global reference, dual-engine. "" at job start.

returns.doc: $REFERENCE (YDB) / $ZREFERENCE (IRIS) â€” the naked indicator

examples: new zz set zz=$data(^VSLTAP("cfg")) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$nakedRef^VSLRPCTAP(),"^VSLTAP(""cfg"")","nakedRef returns the caller's last global reference")
nakedRef.description:
The SVN name differs by engine and neither engine compiles the other's token,
so it is read via XECUTE of an engine-selected assignment â€” no literal SVN in
the compiled routine. XECUTE keeps the current naked-reference context, and a
function call does not reset it, so this returns the CALLER's last reference.
(Engine-neutral â€” a candidate to promote to an m-stdlib STD* primitive later.)

VSLRPCWRAP.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” the XWB broker-dispatch wrap glue (FU-5 / G-RPCHOOK).
VSLRPCWRAP.description:
doc: @exrun bare-ydb
doc: @exsafe transactional
m-lint: disable-file=M-MOD-024
M-MOD-024 false positive: the broker's process-scope vars (XWB/XWBP/XWBSEC/
XWBPTYPE/DUZ/XWBTIP/XWBTSKT) are defined by the CALLER (CALLP^XWBBRK), not by
this routine; the analyser cannot see that cross-frame definition and reads them
as "read before defined". Scalars are $GET-guarded; XWB is walked by $ORDER.

Phase 6 / FU-5. The two fenced side-calls that the active broker `CALLP^XWBBRK`
invokes around its RPC dispatch (the splice is pinned on live foia+vehu source â€”
byte-identical, RPC BROKER; see docs/discoveries/fu-5b-callp-splice.md):

  CALLP^XWBBRK:153  S:$L($G(XWBSEC)) ERR="-1^"_XWBSEC
       :153a  D req^VSLRPCWRAP            ; <- NEW (unconditional): one dir=req per RPC
       :155   IF '+ERR,(+S=0)!(+S>0) D
       :158   . D CAPI^XWBBRK2(.XWBP,XWB(2,"RTAG"),XWB(2,"RNAM"),S)
       :158a  . D resp^VSLRPCWRAP        ; <- NEW (success path only): dir=resp

*** The splice lines are UNCONDITIONAL (`D req^â€¦`, not `D:$G(^global) req^â€¦`): a
global-flag guard in the broker line would itself move the caller's naked indicator
BEFORE the FU-4 fence runs, corrupting the broker's next naked reference. A plain
`DO routine` does not touch the naked indicator, so the wrap entry owns the fence and
gates on `$$captureOn^VSLTAP` from INSIDE it. When the tap is OFF the cost per RPC is
one fence save/restore + the gate read â€” bounded, measured by the 5B non-interference
proof.

Layer: v (VistA-specific: reads XWB* broker vars). It reads ONLY the broker's
process-scope locals (it NEWs nothing the broker owns) and is invisible to the RPC
flow: the FU-4 caller-state fence (naked indicator + $TEST) wraps each entry, and the
ring write is the self-disabling `$$teeRec^VSLTAP`. It never writes a var the broker
depends on â€” the rec it builds is its own local; the only process vars it sets are the
$J-scoped correlation locals VSLWCC/VSLWCID (deliberately VSL-namespaced so a future
XWB patch cannot collide). FU-21 re-validates the splice + the var reads per XWB patch.

Reads at the wrap depth (all confirmed in process scope on live source):
  XWB(2,"CAPI")   the RPC name              XWB(3,"P",*)   the decoded input params
  XWBSEC          set iff CHKPRMIT denied   XWBP           the result slot (post-dispatch)
  XWBPTYPE        #8994 return type 1..4     DUZ / $J       user / job
  XWBTIP:XWBTSKT  client IP:port (EN^XWBTCPC formal params, ancestor frame)

Public API (called only from the CALLP^XWBBRK splice):
  do req^VSLRPCWRAP()    fenced request side-call  (dir=req, denied flag)
  do resp^VSLRPCWRAP()   fenced result side-call   (dir=resp, result by XWBPTYPE)

req.synopsis: Request side-call: emit a dir=req record for EVERY RPC (incl. denied/errored).

returns.doc: fire-and-forget; the broker's result/$ECODE/$T/naked-ref are untouched.

examples: new h,hdr,ok,XWB,XWBSEC tstart ():transactionid="batch" set XWB(2,"CAPI")="ORWPT LIST ALL",XWBSEC="",^VSLTAP("cfg","s3station")="500" do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1),req^VSLRPCWRAP() set h=$$head^VSLTAP(),ok=$$hdr^VSLTAP(h,.hdr) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,hdr("direction"),"req","req emits one dir=req record (rolled back via tstart/trollback)") trollback
req.description:
The FU-4 fence (same pattern as capture^VSLRPCTAP): snapshot $TEST + the naked
indicator BEFORE any global reference, re-establish both on EVERY exit. Owning the
fence HERE lets reqWork read the gate + config (globals) safely (see the file note).

resp.synopsis: Result side-call: emit a dir=resp record on the dispatch-success path.

returns.doc: fenced exactly like req(); correlated to its request by call_id.

examples: new h,hdr,ok,XWB,XWBSEC,XWBP,XWBPTYPE tstart ():transactionid="batch" set XWB(2,"CAPI")="ORWPT LIST ALL",XWBSEC="",XWBP="result",XWBPTYPE=1,^VSLTAP("cfg","s3station")="500" do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1),req^VSLRPCWRAP(),resp^VSLRPCWRAP() set h=$$head^VSLTAP(),ok=$$hdr^VSLTAP(h,.hdr) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,hdr("direction"),"resp","resp emits a dir=resp record on the success path (rolled back)") trollback

reqWork.synopsis: (private) DO-framed: gate, build the dir=req rec from broker vars, tee it.
examples: new h,hdr,ok,XWB,XWBSEC tstart ():transactionid="batch" set XWB(2,"CAPI")="ORWU NEWPERS",XWBSEC="",^VSLTAP("cfg","s3station")="500" do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1),reqWork^VSLRPCWRAP() set h=$$head^VSLTAP(),ok=$$hdr^VSLTAP(h,.hdr) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,hdr("rpc"),"ORWU NEWPERS","reqWork builds the req rec carrying the XWB CAPI rpc name (rolled back)") trollback
reqWork.description:
MUST stay DO-invoked (never `$$`): the trap's arg-less QUIT raises M17
NOTEXTRINSIC in an extrinsic frame (the append/write1 gotcha).

params.synopsis: (private) join the broker's decoded input params XWB(3,"P",*) verbatim (no typing â€” Â§9).

returns.doc: the params $C(1)-joined in subscript order (FU-16(c): inputs

examples: new XWB set XWB(3,"P",1)="ALPHA",XWB(3,"P",2)="9",XWB(3,"P",3)="" do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$params^VSLRPCWRAP(),"ALPHA"_$char(1)_"9"_$char(1)_"","params $C(1)-joined verbatim in subscript order")
params.description:
land in XWB(3,"P",*); there is no single param array to grab). A LOCAL $ORDER â€”
it does not touch the naked indicator.

result.synopsis: (private) classify the result by XWBPTYPE (FU-16(c)) â€” scalar payload or a snapshot ref.
examples: new rec,XWBPTYPE,XWBP set XWBPTYPE=1,XWBP="hello" do result^VSLRPCWRAP(.rec) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,rec("result_kind"),"scalar","XWBPTYPE=1 -> result_kind=scalar")
new rec,XWBPTYPE,XWBP set XWBPTYPE=1,XWBP="hello" do result^VSLRPCWRAP(.rec) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,rec("payload"),"hello","scalar payload = the verbatim XWBP value")

new rec,XWBPTYPE,XWBP set XWBPTYPE=4,XWBP="^TMP($J,""X"")" do result^VSLRPCWRAP(.rec) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,rec("result_kind"),"global","XWBPTYPE=4 -> result_kind=global, gref = the closed-root ref")
new rec,XWBPTYPE set XWBPTYPE=2 do result^VSLRPCWRAP(.rec) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,rec("gref"),"XWBP","XWBPTYPE=2 (table) -> gref=XWBP (a LOCAL array tree)")
result.description:
XWBPTYPE 1=single -> scalar (XWBP IS the value); 4=global array -> XWBP is a
closed-root global ref string (gref=its value); 2=table/3=WP -> XWBP is a LOCAL
array tree (gref="XWBP" â€” write1rec MERGEs @gref). The MERGE is FU-17's one in-path op.

ctx.synopsis: (private) FU-18 context read at the wrap depth (process scope; ancestor EN^XWBTCPC).
examples: new rec,DUZ,XWBTIP,XWBTSKT set DUZ=168,XWBTIP="10.1.2.3",XWBTSKT=51001 do ctx^VSLRPCWRAP(.rec) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,rec("client"),"10.1.2.3:51001","client = XWBTIP:XWBTSKT (FU-18)")
new rec,DUZ,XWBTIP,XWBTSKT set DUZ=168 do ctx^VSLRPCWRAP(.rec) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,rec("duz"),168,"duz read from DUZ; job = $job")
ctx.description:
DUZ/$J + the client IP:port (XWBTIP:XWBTSKT, EN^XWBTCPC formal params â€” OPEN-2,
read the vars directly, not the truncated procname). All LOCAL reads (no global).

VSLS3.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” S3 egress sink: LDJSON envelope + the Â§11 bucket layout.
VSLS3.description:
doc: @exrun bare
m-lint: disable-file=M-MOD-024
M-MOD-024 false positive: `hdr` is populated by reference by $$hdr^VSLTAP in
resolveRec; the analyser cannot see through the by-ref call, so it reads it as
"read before defined".
m-lint: disable-file=M-MOD-036
M-MOD-036: gSerialize walks a v2 "g" MERGE snapshot via @root/@nref indirection.
The refs are internal closed ^XTMP("VSLTAP",â€¦) names built by $name here (drain-side,
off the in-path), never external input â€” the indirection is the deferred FU-17 serialize.

Phase 3 / M2 of the RPC+HL7 -> S3 traffic tap (spec Â§9/Â§11/Â§12). VSLS3 is
the `v` wrapper over the `m` S3 client (STDS3/STDSIGV4): it frames one
verbatim captured record as a single LDJSON line carrying the RAW payload
(escaped-inline, lossless and greppable â€” base64 a per-stream switch), and
it PUTs/GETs objects under the per-station key layout. The envelope is the
ENTIRE format decision (Â§9): a thin, non-interpretive header (ts, proto,
dir, station, seq, conn, len) + the RAW payload. **No parsing, typing, or
structuring of the traffic happens here** â€” the only operations are
content-preserving (copy -> envelope -> ship). NO hashing/integrity field is
added: RPC traffic is plain ASCII the tap only OBSERVES, and fidelity is
proven downstream by BYTE-EQUALITY against the captured source (VSLTAPFC), not
by an embedded digest. In-transit/at-rest integrity is the storage layer's
(S3's) job, not the tap's.

*** Layer: v (above the m/v waterline). It consumes `m` DOWN only â€”
STDJSON (the public node->text encoder, so JSON escaping is never hand-
rolled), STDB64 (the base64 switch), STDDATE (the day
partition), and STDS3/STDSIGV4 (the egress transport monopoly, the only
way to reach S3). It never inverts the dependency. The S3 credentials /
endpoint / bucket / station are a CONFIG SEAM read from ^VSLTAP("cfg",â€¦)
(the same control state the Phase-2 core uses), so a deployment points the
sink at real S3 or a local S3-equivalent (MinIO/LocalStack) with no code
change â€” just the `endpoint` override (Â§15.1).

The live PUT/GET round-trip needs engine HTTP egress (G-HTTP-YDB /
G-HTTP-IRIS-GET); the envelope/key/config core here is egress-INDEPENDENT
and runs on a bare engine. The round-trip is proven in the integration
harness against a MinIO sink (spec Â§15.2).

Public API:
  $$envelope(.rec,.opt)                      one schema-v1 LDJSON line from a field array
  $$gSerialize(seq)                          serialize a v2 "g" MERGE subtree (FU-17, drain-side)
  $$key(station,proto,seq,ymd)               traffic/<st>/<proto>/Y/M/D/<seq>.ndjson
  $$offWindowsKey(station,ymd)               traffic/<st>/_offwindows/Y/M/D.json
  $$fidelityKey(station,ymd)                 traffic/<st>/_fidelity/Y/M/D.json
  $$ctx(ctx,opt)                             build the S3 ctx+opt from the seam -> bucket
  $$ship(ctx,bucket,key,body,opt,resp)       PUT one object (status; egress)
  $$readback(ctx,bucket,key,opt,resp)        GET one object (status; egress)
  $$list(ctx,bucket,prefix,opt,listing)      LIST object keys under a prefix (status; egress)
  $$drain(res)                               flush the ^XTMP ring -> ship -> trim

envelope.synopsis: Frame one captured record as a single schema-v1 LDJSON line.

params.name: rec
params.doc:
by-ref schema-v1 field array; rec("payload") = the RAW bytes,
plus event_id/call_id/ts/protocol/direction/station/seq/rpc/
duz/job/client[/denied|result_kind]/chunk_count/payload_encoding

returns.doc: one schema-v1 JSON object line, no trailing newline

examples: new rec,opt,line,t set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-7",rec("seq")=7,rec("payload")="hello world" set line=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$parse^STDJSON(line,.t),"the line is well-formed JSON") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$valueOf^STDJSON(t("payload")),"hello world","payload round-trips byte-exact")

new rec,opt,line,t set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-7",rec("seq")=7,rec("payload")="x" set line=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$parse^STDJSON(line,.t),"well-formed") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$valueOf^STDJSON(t("event_id")),"500-1-7:resp","event_id = call_id ':' direction")

new rec,opt,line,t set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-7",rec("seq")=7,rec("payload")="hello world" set line=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$parse^STDJSON(line,.t),"well-formed") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$valueOf^STDJSON(t("wire_len")),11,"wire_len = byte length of the RAW payload")

new rec,opt,line,t set rec("direction")="req",rec("call_id")="500-1-8",rec("seq")=8,rec("denied")=1,rec("payload")="p" set line=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$parse^STDJSON(line,.t),"well-formed") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$valueOf^STDJSON(t("denied")),1,"a req carries denied, not result_kind")

new rec,opt,line,t set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-9",rec("seq")=9,rec("payload")="v" set line=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$parse^STDJSON(line,.t),"well-formed") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$valueOf^STDJSON(t("result_kind")),"scalar","a resp carries result_kind, not denied")

new rec,opt,line,t set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-7",rec("seq")=7,rec("payload")=("a"_$char(9)_"b"_""""_"q") set opt("base64")=1 set line=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$parse^STDJSON(line,.t),"well-formed") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$decode^STDB64($$valueOf^STDJSON(t("payload"))),"a"_$char(9)_"b"_""""_"q","base64 switch decodes byte-exact")

new rec,opt,a,b set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-7",rec("seq")=7,rec("payload")="hello world" set a=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt),b=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,a,b,"deterministic: same fixture frames byte-identical (M-collation key order)")
envelope.description:
The object is assembled as a STDJSON node and serialised by the PUBLIC
$$encode^STDJSON â€” so the payload's JSON string-escaping is lossless and never
hand-rolled. Keys emit in M collation order (deterministic, so fixtures
byte-compare). ONE shape serves req and resp (`direction` discriminates): a req
carries `denied`, a resp carries `result_kind`. wire_len is computed HERE over
the RAW bytes; NO payload digest is emitted (fidelity is proven by byte-equality
downstream in VSLTAPFC, not an embedded hash). RAW is byte-faithful + greppable and round-trips
under byte mode (1 M char == 1 byte); set opt("base64") (the Â§9 per-stream switch,
FU-1 default) for guaranteed-conformant UTF-8 output on binary streams.
Full contract: docs/design/s3tap-envelope-schema-lock.md Â§3 (schema v1).

gSerialize.synopsis: Serialize a v2 GLOBAL-ARRAY MERGE snapshot (^...,"g") to a deterministic, lossless blob.

params.name: seq
params.doc: the ring sequence of a v2 record whose result_kind="global"

returns.doc: one node per line: $$encode^STDJSON of {s:<subscripts>,v:<value>}

examples: new save,b merge save=^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",991,"g") kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",991,"g") set ^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",991,"g","DPT",1)="ONE",^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",991,"g","DPT",2)="TWO" set b=$$gSerialize^VSLS3(991) kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",991,"g") merge ^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",991,"g")=save do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$length(b,$char(10)),2,"one JSON node line per descendant of the g MERGE snapshot")
gSerialize.description:
FU-17 defers ALL of a global result's serialize/chunk/hash to the drain (off the
in-path; capture did one MERGE). The $QUERY walk is in M-collation order so the
blob is deterministic across engines, and STDJSON escaping makes each node lossless
for arbitrary bytes (the blob is then base64'd in the envelope). The exact wire form
(vs the broker's SNDDATA encoding) is FU-11's concern; here it need only be a
deterministic, byte-faithful representation the Â§15.2 round-trip can prove.

key.synopsis: The object key for one traffic stream: traffic/<st>/<proto>/Y/M/D/<seq>.ndjson.

returns.doc: the S3 object key

offWindowsKey.synopsis: The per-day _offwindows manifest key (explicit tap-off windows, Â§11).

returns.doc: the S3 object key

examples: write $$offWindowsKey^VSLS3("500","20260619")  ; "traffic/500/_offwindows/2026/06/19.json"

fidelityKey.synopsis: The per-day _fidelity manifest key (periodic VSLTAPFC results, Â§11).

returns.doc: the S3 object key

examples: write $$fidelityKey^VSLS3("500","20260619")  ; "traffic/500/_fidelity/2026/06/19.json"

ctx.synopsis: Build the S3 credential ctx + opt(endpoint) from the ^VSLTAP config seam.

returns.doc: the bucket name from the seam

examples: new ctx,opt,b,save merge save=^VSLTAP("cfg") kill ^VSLTAP("cfg") set ^VSLTAP("cfg","s3bucket")="vista-traffic",^VSLTAP("cfg","s3endpoint")="http://m-s3-minio:9000" set b=$$ctx^VSLS3(.ctx,.opt) kill ^VSLTAP("cfg") merge ^VSLTAP("cfg")=save do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,b,"vista-traffic","bucket + endpoint read from the ^VSLTAP config seam (set-then-restore)")
ctx.description:
The same control state the Phase-2 core uses (^VSLTAP("cfg",â€¦)). An
empty endpoint -> virtual-hosted real S3; a set endpoint -> path-style
S3-equivalent (the no-code-change testbed hook, Â§15.1).

ship.synopsis: PUT one object to S3 / the S3-equivalent via STDS3.

params.name: body
params.doc: the LDJSON body (one or more envelope lines)

returns.doc: HTTP status (200 ok); 0 on transport failure

ship.description:
The ONLY way to reach S3 (waterline rule 3: the m S3 client is the
transport monopoly). Runs in the SEPARATE flush process, never the
RPC path (Â§4.1.3). 0 on a bare engine with no HTTP egress (G-HTTP-*).
ship.illustrative: live S3/MinIO PUT over engine HTTP egress (STDS3); no side-effect-safe bare-engine example â€” proven in the Â§15.2 MinIO integration harness.

readback.synopsis: GET one object back from S3 / the S3-equivalent via STDS3.

params.name: opt
params.doc: by-ref: opt("endpoint") â€” REQUIRED to reach the S3-equivalent

params.name: resp
params.doc: OUT by-ref: resp("body") holds the bytes on 200

returns.doc: HTTP status (200 ok); 0 on transport failure

readback.description:
The fidelity-harness read leg (spec Â§15.2 step 4): read the shipped
object back and compare byte-for-byte (VSLTAPFC). `opt` carries the
endpoint override (from $$ctx) so the GET reaches the same endpoint the
PUT used â€” without it the read op signs+sends to real AWS, not MinIO.
readback.illustrative: live S3/MinIO GET over engine HTTP egress (STDS3); the read leg of the Â§15.2 round-trip, not runnable on a bare engine.

list.synopsis: LIST object keys under `prefix` via STDS3 listObjectsV2.

params.name: prefix
params.doc: the key prefix to list under ("" = whole bucket)

params.name: opt
params.doc: by-ref: opt("endpoint") â€” REQUIRED to reach the S3-equivalent

returns.doc: HTTP status (200 ok); 0 on transport failure

list.description:
The discovery leg for the periodic fidelity sampler (VSLTAPRUN
$$fidelityNow): enumerate recently-shipped objects under the per-station
prefix so they can be read back and integrity-verified. Same `opt`
endpoint override as ship/readback. LIST is dual-engine-proven (STDS3MINIOTST).
list.illustrative: live S3/MinIO LIST over engine HTTP egress (STDS3 listObjectsV2); proven in STDS3MINIOTST, not on a bare engine.

drain.synopsis: Flush the ^XTMP ring to S3 as one LDJSON batch, then trim the shipped entries.

returns.doc: the number of records shipped (0 if gated/empty/failed)

examples: new res,save,csave merge save=^XTMP("VSLTAP") merge csave=^VSLTAP("cfg") kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP"),^VSLTAP("cfg") set ^VSLTAP("cfg","s3sink")="capture",^VSLTAP("cfg","mode")="armed",^VSLTAP("cfg","consumer")=1,^VSLTAP("cfg","s3station")="500" set ^XTMP("VSLTAP","head")=1,^XTMP("VSLTAP","tail")=0,^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",1)="ONELINE" set n=$$drain^VSLS3(.res) kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP"),^VSLTAP("cfg") merge ^XTMP("VSLTAP")=save merge ^VSLTAP("cfg")=csave do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,n,1,"capture-sink seam drains the 1-record ring without a live PUT (status 200)")
drain.description:
The whole egress path runs in the SEPARATE flush process (VSLTASK calls
this) â€” never the RPC CPU (Â§4.1.3). It is consumer-gated AND auto-
failover-aware: it ships ONLY while the Phase-2 gate is enabled, so a
dead/slow sink or any interference signal turns it off cleanly (the gate
records the off-window). On a 200 it self-drains the shipped seqs
(drainTo^VSLTAP); on any other status it leaves the ring for retry.

resolveRec.synopsis: Build the schema-v1 field array for the record at `seq` (dual-mode: v2 header / v1 legacy).

params.name: station
params.doc: the station partition (authoritative for the Â§11 key)

params.name: proto
params.doc: default protocol when the record carries none

params.name: erec
params.doc: OUT by-ref: the schema-v1 fields incl. erec("payload") = RAW bytes

examples: new erec,save merge save=^XTMP("VSLTAP") kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") set ^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",993)="RAWBYTES" do resolveRec^VSLS3(993,"500","rpc",.erec) kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") merge ^XTMP("VSLTAP")=save do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,erec("payload"),"RAWBYTES","a legacy v1 record resolves to a plain payload under the station/proto key (set-then-restore)")
resolveRec.description:
A v2 record (the FU-5 wrap) is framed from its 18-piece header: a GLOBAL ARRAY
result is serialized from the "g" MERGE snapshot (FU-17) and base64'd; a scalar
from its "p",1 chunk. A legacy v1 string record ($$append â€” the synthetic
demonstrator path) ships as a plain payload under the configured direction. The
header's schema_version keeps both readable from one ring (schema-lock Â§4).

VSLSEC.description:
Binds the VistA *authorization decision* â€” the part of the security seam
that has NO portable analog and so cannot live below the waterline. Three
bindings, each VistA-only:
  - the security-key check ($$hasKey, over Kernel's ^XUSEC);
  - the ambient principal ($$duz, the NEW PERSON #200 IEN);
  - the principal -> #200 NAME resolution ($$user), which REUSES VSLFS
    (v->v composition; no FileMan DBS re-bind â€” waterline Â§9 no-duplication
    applies within `v` too).

*** NO portable crypto here â€” STDCRYPTO owns it. *** Portable token crypto
(SHA digests, HMAC, constant-time compare) lives in STDCRYPTO (libcrypto on
YDB / $SYSTEM.Encryption on IRIS, dual-engine proven) and is called up by a
consumer that needs it. VSLSEC binds NO Kernel hash back end: grounded
2026-06-16, there is no portable Kernel generic-hash entry point â€”
$$SHAHASH^XUSHSH is absent on the YDB-VistA test engine (pre XU*8.0*655)
and the classic top-level ^XUSHSH returns a constant on both engines. The
architecture (Â§3.4) is explicit: "Portable token crypto stays in STD*; the
VistA authorization decision lives in VSL." This module is that decision.

Public API:
  $$hasKey^VSLSEC(key,duz) â€” 1 iff `duz` holds security key `key`, else 0
  $$duz^VSLSEC()           â€” the ambient principal (+$GET(DUZ), the #200 IEN)
  $$user^VSLSEC(duz)       â€” the #200 NAME for `duz` (via VSLFS), else ""
  $$lastError^VSLSEC()     â€” last error detail, else ""

*** ERROR CONTRACT â€” loud on a malformed call, never on a normal DENY ***
An authorization DENY is a normal `0` from $$hasKey â€” NOT an error. A
malformed call (an empty key name) maps to a clean ,U-VSL-SEC-ARG, $ECODE,
with the detail in ^TMP($job,"vslsec","err") for $$lastError. This mirrors
VSLFS's loud-failure posture (a real fault is loud; an absent value is not).

ICR note: ^XUSEC is the documented Supported *reference* for security-key
membership ("do not reference the SECURITY KEY (#19.1) file ... check the
^XUSEC global ... this is (and continues to be) a supported reference" â€”
Kernel DG, Security Keys). It carries no numeric DBIA in the gold corpus, so
the call is tagged with the notional ICR marker (a read, never a write â€” the
no-direct-global rule forbids set/kill, not the Supported $D reference). See
docs/memory notional-dbia-not-a-blocker + plan Â§5.4.

hasKey.synopsis: 1 iff `duz` (default: the ambient DUZ) holds security key `key`.

returns.doc: 1 iff the user holds the key; 0 (a normal DENY) otherwise

raises.doc: the call is malformed (an empty key name)

examples: do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$hasKey^VSLSEC("ZZ NO SUCH KEY",1),0,"hasKey is 0 (a normal DENY) for an unheld key")

new k,d set k=$order(^XUSEC("")),d=$select(k'="":$order(^XUSEC(k,0)),1:"") do:k'=""&(d'="") eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$hasKey^VSLSEC(k,d),1,"hasKey is 1 for an existing held ^XUSEC(key,duz) pair (probed read-only)") do:k=""!(d="") true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,1,"no ^XUSEC pairs present (bare engine) - held-key path verified on vehu/foia")
do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set x=$$hasKey^VSLSEC("""",1)","U-VSL-SEC-ARG","$$hasKey with an empty key raises U-VSL-SEC-...")

duz.synopsis: The ambient principal â€” +$GET(DUZ), the caller's NEW PERSON (#200) IEN.

returns.doc: the ambient DUZ (0 when no signon context is set)

examples: new DUZ set DUZ=1 do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$duz^VSLSEC(),1,"$$duz returns the ambient DUZ (NEWed, no side effect)")

user.synopsis: The #200 NAME for `duz` (default: the ambient DUZ), resolved via VSLFS.

returns.doc: the NEW PERSON (#200) .01 NAME, or "" if absent

user.description:
Reuses $$get^VSLFS (FileMan DBS) â€” the principal->#200 binding without
re-binding the DBS (v->v composition; waterline Â§9 no-duplication).
user.illustrative: resolves the #200 NAME via $$GET1^DIQ (FileMan DBS) â€” exercised on live VistA by VSLSECTST; not a portable self-contained one-liner (faults on a bare engine where $$GET1^DIQ is absent, and on a FileMan-restricted live instance)

bySecid.synopsis: The #200 IEN for a SecID via EN1^XUPSQRY (RPC XUPS PERSONQUERY), else "".

params.name: secid
params.doc: the IAM Security ID (SECID, NEW PERSON #200 field #205.1)

returns.doc: the #200 IEN bound to that SecID, or "" if none / not on a VistA engine

raises.doc: the call is malformed (an empty SecID)

examples: do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set x=$$bySecid^VSLSEC("""")","U-VSL-SEC-ARG","$$bySecid("""") raises U-VSL-SEC-...")

do:$text(EN1^XUPSQRY)'="" eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$bySecid^VSLSEC("ZZNO-SUCH-SECID-99999"),"","an unprovisioned SecID resolves to no #200 IEN") do:$text(EN1^XUPSQRY)="" true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,1,"EN1^XUPSQRY absent (bare engine) - SecID lookup verified on vehu/foia")
bySecid.description:
@icr 4575 @call EN1^XUPSQRY @status Controlled Subscription @custodian XU @source XU/krn_8_0_dg_common_services_ug#en1xupsqry-query-new-person-file
The SSOi/2FA identity binding: a federated subject (the SecID claim of a
validated token) -> the local #200 IEN, the way Kernel's own XUSAML/
XUESSO2 resolve an STS SAML token. EN1^XUPSQRY queries #200 by SecID
(param 2; null last name in param 3) and stuffs a by-ref result array
(result(1)=1/0 found-flag; result(1,0)=VPID^IEN^name~...). On a bare
engine (EN1^XUPSQRY absent) this returns "" â€” the caller $text-gates the
live path. No direct ^VA(200 read: the lookup is the Controlled-
Subscription API and the IEN is read from the returned array (waterline).

lastError.synopsis: The last VSLSEC error message (the composed malformed-call detail).

returns.doc: ^TMP($job,"vslsec","err"), or "" if none

examples: new $etrap set $etrap="set $ecode=""""" do hasKey^VSLSEC("") do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$lastError^VSLSEC()'="","lastError carries the malformed-call detail after a loud failure")

VSLTAP.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” non-interference traffic-tap core (the safety gate).
VSLTAP.description:
doc: @exrun bare

Phase 2 / M1 of the RPC+HL7 -> S3 traffic tap (spec Â§6/Â§4.1). VSLTAP is the
load-bearing capture core everything downstream waits behind. Its whole job
is to be INVISIBLE to the clinical RPC flow it observes: a bounded, rolling
^XTMP ring filled by an irreducible memory-copy append. Post-FU-9 the gates are
SPLIT: the ring CAPTURES whenever armed and not auto-failed-over (always-on,
`$$captureOn`), and only EGRESS is consumer/sink-gated (`$$enabled`); it is fenced
so any fault self-disables instead of touching the caller, and watched so ANY
interference signal flips the tap OFF automatically (fail-safe-OFF).

*** Layer: v (above the m/v waterline). It touches ^XTMP (Kernel's SAC-
sanctioned scratch global) and consumes `m` utilities DOWN (STDDATE for the
FileMan purge-date); it never inverts the dependency. The egress/VistA
bindings (XPAR config source, VSLRPC chokepoint, Kernel XQ82 purge schedule)
are SEAMS, not Phase-2 dependencies â€” so the gate runs on a BARE engine with
no VistA and no S3 (kickoff). Config/state ride in ^VSLTAP (small control
state); the rolling capture cache rides in ^XTMP("VSLTAP",â€¦) (the auto-
purged scratch global, Â§4.1.1 â€” no FileMan file).

Capture buffer (the rolling "flight recorder", Â§4.1, Option A / D-10):
  ^XTMP("VSLTAP",0)            purgedate^createdate^description (Kernel XQ82)
  ^XTMP("VSLTAP","head")       highest written seq
  ^XTMP("VSLTAP","tail")       (lowest-retained seq) - 1
  ^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",seq)   the verbatim record (no transform)

Control state (^VSLTAP â€” VSL namespace):
  ^VSLTAP("cfg",key)           mode/consumer/alwayson/cap/maxbytes/latbound/â€¦
  ^VSLTAP("disabled")          auto-failover reason, else absent
  ^VSLTAP("hb")                liveness heartbeat ($H)
  ^VSLTAP("_offwindows")       count; (,n)=open^reason^close (explicit, never silent)
  ^VSLTAP("hl",â€¦)              health counters/samples (VSLTAPHL)
  ^VSLTAP("fc","last")         last fidelity manifest line (VSLTAPFC persist)

Public API:
  $$captureOn^VSLTAP()         1 iff the ring should capture now (always-on; FU-9)
  $$enabled^VSLTAP()           1 iff egress should run now (capture-on + consumer)
  $$append^VSLTAP(rec)         gated memory-copy append -> 1 captured / 0 not
  $$tee^VSLTAP(rec)            fault-fenced $$append (used by VSLRPCTAP)
  $$appendRec^VSLTAP(.rec)     gated append of a RICH record (cache layout v2; FU-5)
  $$teeRec^VSLTAP(.rec)        fault-fenced $$appendRec (used by the FU-5 wrap)
  $$hdr^VSLTAP(seq,.out)       parse the v2 header at seq -> out(field); 1 iff v2
  $$isV2^VSLTAP(seq) / $$chunk^VSLTAP(seq,i)   v2 record classify / payload chunk
  $$size^VSLTAP() / $$head() / $$tail() / $$read(seq) / $$present(seq)   ring inspection
  $$state^VSLTAP()             OFF | ARMED-IDLE | ACTIVE | AUTO-DISABLED | UNHEALTHY
  $$disabled^VSLTAP()          auto-failover reason or ""
  do arm() / off() / setConsumer(b) / setAlwaysOn(b)   operator/gate controls
  do disable(reason) / rearm()  auto-failover OFF / re-arm (records _offwindows)
  do heartbeat() / $$healthy()  liveness
  do purgeNode()               write the Kernel auto-purge node
  $$cfg(key,default)           read a config knob (consumed by VSLTAPHL)
  $$offWindows(.out)           recorded off-windows -> count

cfg.synopsis: Read a config knob from ^VSLTAP("cfg",key), else `default`.

returns.doc: the configured value or the default

examples: do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$cfg^VSLTAP("nosuchkey","fallback"),"fallback","unset key returns the default")
set ^VSLTAP("cfg","cap")=750 do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$cfg^VSLTAP("cap",1000),750,"a set cfg knob reads back") kill ^VSLTAP("cfg","cap")

arm.synopsis: Operator: arm the tap (kill-switch ON) and clear any prior auto-disable.
examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$cfg^VSLTAP("mode","off"),"armed","arm sets mode=armed") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP set ^VSLTAP("disabled")="fault" do arm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$disabled^VSLTAP(),"","arm clears a prior auto-disable") kill ^VSLTAP

off.synopsis: Operator: kill-switch OFF (state OFF; capture cannot run).
examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),off^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$state^VSLTAP(),"OFF","kill-switch -> state OFF") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),off^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$captureOn^VSLTAP(),0,"OFF -> capture gate cannot run") kill ^VSLTAP

setConsumer.synopsis: Set the consumer-presence flag (D-5): no consumer -> egress/capture OFF.
examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$enabled^VSLTAP(),1,"consumer present -> egress enabled") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(0) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$enabled^VSLTAP(),0,"no consumer -> egress gated off") kill ^VSLTAP

setAlwaysOn.synopsis: LEGACY/SUBSUMED (D-8 -> FU-9): kept for backward compatibility; no longer gates capture.
examples: kill ^VSLTAP do setAlwaysOn^VSLTAP(1) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$cfg^VSLTAP("alwayson",0),1,"the legacy flag is still written/readable") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),setAlwaysOn^VSLTAP(0) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$captureOn^VSLTAP(),1,"SUBSUMED: alwayson=0 no longer gates the always-on ring") kill ^VSLTAP
setAlwaysOn.description:
The ring is ALWAYS-ON by default now ($$captureOn), so this opt-in is a no-op
for gating. The cfg key is still written/readable so existing callers (e.g. the
v-web console display) keep resolving; remove once consumers migrate.

seed.synopsis: Populate ^VSLTAP("cfg",â€¦) from the installed XPAR #8989.51 params (self-configuring install).
seed.description:
The KIDS install creates the tap params (VSLTAPBO $$params); the hot-path
gate ($$cfg) and the VSLS3 ctx seam read ^VSLTAP("cfg",â€¦), NOT XPAR (no
XPAR/FileMan read on the capture path). seed bridges the two: it copies
each set param into its cfg key once, at install/configure time. The
fidelity cadence is read from XPAR directly (VSLTAPRUN), so it is not
mirrored here. $text(GET^XPAR)-guarded -> a bare engine is a clean no-op.
@icr 2263 @call $$GET^XPAR @status Supported @custodian XU @source XU/krn_8_0_dg_toolkit_ug#getxpar-return-an-instance-of-a-parameter
seed.illustrative: reads the installed XPAR #8989.51 tap params via $$GET^XPAR (live Kernel); on a bare engine $text(GET^XPAR)="" makes it a clean no-op, so a meaningful example needs an installed VistA with the params set.

seedMap.synopsis: Map each installed XPAR param name to the ^VSLTAP("cfg") key the tap reads; return the count.

params.name: map
params.doc: OUT by-ref: map(i,"param")=XPAR name, map(i,"cfg")=cfg key

returns.doc: the number of param->cfg mappings (the fidelity cadence is read direct)

examples: new m do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$seedMap^VSLTAP(.m),9,"nine param->cfg mappings")
new m,n set n=$$seedMap^VSLTAP(.m) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,m(1,"param")_"="_m(1,"cfg"),"VSL TAP CAP=cap","first row maps the CAP param to the cap key")

captureOn.synopsis: FU-9 (D-6): 1 iff the RING should capture now â€” armed AND not auto-disabled.

returns.doc: the ALWAYS-ON capture gate. The ring records whenever the tap

examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$captureOn^VSLTAP(),1,"armed + clean -> capture ON regardless of consumer") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),disable^VSLTAP("fault") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$captureOn^VSLTAP(),0,"auto-failover -> capture OFF (fail-safe)") kill ^VSLTAP
captureOn.description:
is armed and not auto-failed-over, INDEPENDENT of any consumer/sink â€” a down or
absent sink pauses only EGRESS ($$enabled), never capture (the flight-recorder
keeps a window of traffic ready for whenever a consumer attaches; it laps to
drop_oldest only under sustained pressure). The ONLY things that stop the ring
are the operator kill-switch ($$off) and auto-failover ($$disable) â€” fail-safe-OFF.

enabled.synopsis: 1 iff EGRESS should run now: capture-on AND a consumer/sink is present (D-5).

returns.doc: the EGRESS gate. The drain ships and $$state reports ACTIVE only

examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$enabled^VSLTAP(),1,"capture-on + consumer -> egress enabled") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$enabled^VSLTAP(),0,"capture-on but no consumer -> egress gated off") kill ^VSLTAP
enabled.description:
when this holds; with no consumer the ring still captures ($$captureOn) but the
PUT pauses (the off-window is recorded, never silent). [FU-9 split the former
single gate into capture (always-on) vs egress (consumer-gated).] The legacy
`alwayson` flag is SUBSUMED â€” the ring is always-on by default now â€” and no
longer gates anything (the setter/cfg are kept for backward compatibility).

append.synopsis: Gated, fault-fenced, bounded memory-copy append of a verbatim record.

params.name: rec
params.doc: the verbatim payload (no parse, no transform â€” D-1)

returns.doc: 1 iff captured; 0 iff gated, auto-disabled, copy-cost-tripped, or fenced

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$append^VSLTAP("rpc-record"),1,"captures with no consumer (always-on ring)") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")

kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$append^VSLTAP("TST^DUZ=1") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$read^VSLTAP($$head^VSLTAP()),"TST^DUZ=1","the stored record is verbatim (no transform)") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do off^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$append^VSLTAP("x"),0,"OFF -> gated, no append") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
append.description:
The ONLY in-path op (besides the $G gate): one global SET + bounded trim.
No LOCK, no serialize, no socket, no I/O, no block (Â§6.1). Self-fenced:
ANY fault inside the write self-disables and returns 0 â€” never the caller.
Flag-based $ETRAP (ISO, dual-engine; NEVER zgoto, the M4 resident-harness
gotcha). The write runs in a DO-invoked frame (write1) so the trap's
argument-less QUIT is legal â€” an arg-less QUIT trap fired in an extrinsic
($$) frame raises M17 NOTEXTRINSIC (per STDASSERT raises()).

tee.synopsis: The named capture seam the VSLRPC chokepoint calls (VSLRPCTAP) â€” fenced.

returns.doc: the $$append result (0 if gated or a fault was fenced)

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$tee^VSLTAP("rec"),1,"tee adapts to $$append -> 1 when armed") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do off^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$tee^VSLTAP("rec"),0,"tee returns 0 when gated off") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
tee.description:
$$append is already self-fenced (a DO-framed $ETRAP that self-disables on
any fault); tee is the stable adapter entry. Either leaves the caller's
result / $ECODE / $T untouched.

appendRec.synopsis: FU-5: gated, fault-fenced, bounded append of a RICH (cache layout v2) record.

returns.doc: 1 iff captured; 0 iff gated, auto-disabled, copy-cost-tripped, or fenced

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") set rec("dir")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-1",rec("rpc")="ORWPT INFO",rec("payload")="body",rec("result_kind")="scalar" do arm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$appendRec^VSLTAP(.rec),1,"a rich v2 record is captured when armed") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") set rec("payload")="body" do off^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$appendRec^VSLTAP(.rec),0,"OFF -> gated, no v2 append") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
appendRec.description:
The v2 sibling of $$append â€” same self-disabling $ETRAP, same DO-framed write
(write1rec) so the trap's arg-less QUIT is legal. The caller-state fence
(naked-ref/$TEST) lives one level up in capture^VSLRPCTAP (FU-4).

teeRec.synopsis: The named rich-record capture seam the FU-5 wrap calls (via VSLRPCTAP) â€” fenced.

returns.doc: the $$appendRec result (0 if gated or a fault was fenced)

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") set rec("payload")="b",rec("result_kind")="scalar" do arm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$teeRec^VSLTAP(.rec),1,"teeRec adapts to $$appendRec -> 1 when armed") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") set rec("payload")="b" do off^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$teeRec^VSLTAP(.rec),0,"teeRec returns 0 when gated off") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$append^VSLTAP("a") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$size^VSLTAP(),1,"one record -> size 1") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$size^VSLTAP(),0,"empty ring -> size 0")
kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$head^VSLTAP(),0,"empty ring -> head 0")
kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$append^VSLTAP("a"),zz=$$append^VSLTAP("b") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$head^VSLTAP(),2,"two appends -> head 2") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$tail^VSLTAP(),0,"empty ring -> tail 0")

kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() set ^VSLTAP("cfg","cap")=2,zz=$$append^VSLTAP("r1"),zz=$$append^VSLTAP("r2"),zz=$$append^VSLTAP("r3"),zz=$$append^VSLTAP("r4") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$tail^VSLTAP(),2,"cap=2 after 4 appends -> tail advanced to 2 (oldest 2 dropped)") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")

read.synopsis: The verbatim record at `seq`, or "" if absent/overwritten.
examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$append^VSLTAP("hello") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$read^VSLTAP(1),"hello","reads back the verbatim record at seq 1") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$read^VSLTAP(99),"","absent seq -> empty string")

present.synopsis: 1 iff a data node exists at `seq` ($DATA'=0) â€” distinguishes an empty-string record from an absent/uncommitted slot.

returns.doc: The drain ships only the CONTIGUOUS COMMITTED prefix using this:

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$append^VSLTAP("x") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$present^VSLTAP(1),1,"a committed slot is present") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$present^VSLTAP(99),0,"an absent slot is not present")
present.description:
FU-8's atomic $INCREMENT advances head one statement BEFORE the data SET, so a
concurrent always-on drain (FU-9) can momentarily see head ahead of an in-flight
slot; stopping at the first absent node leaves that slot for the next tick rather
than shipping "" and trimming a record that is about to land. `$$read` can't tell
an absent slot from a legitimately-empty record ($get-> "" for both); this can.

isV2.synopsis: 1 iff the record at `seq` is a cache-layout-v2 record (a "p" or "g" child present).
examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$append^VSLTAP("flat") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$isV2^VSLTAP(1),0,"a legacy v1 string record is not v2") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")

kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") set zr=$name(^TMP($job,"V2")),@zr@("a")="x",rec("dir")="resp",rec("gref")=zr,rec("result_kind")="global" do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$appendRec^VSLTAP(.rec) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$isV2^VSLTAP($$head^VSLTAP()),"a record with a g child is v2") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP"),@zr
isV2.description: A legacy v1 string record ($$append) has only the scalar data node â€” no child.

hdr.synopsis: Parse the v2 header at `seq` into out("schema_version"/...); return 1 iff a v2 record.

params.name: out
params.doc: OUT by-ref: the 18 header fields keyed by their schema-v1 names

returns.doc: 1 iff `seq` is a v2 record (else `out` is killed, returns 0)

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$append^VSLTAP("flat") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$hdr^VSLTAP(1,.zo),0,"a v1 record has no v2 header -> 0") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")

kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") set zr=$name(^TMP($job,"V2")),@zr@("a")="x",rec("dir")="resp",rec("rpc")="ORWU GLOBAL",rec("gref")=zr,rec("result_kind")="global" do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$appendRec^VSLTAP(.rec) set zz=$$hdr^VSLTAP($$head^VSLTAP(),.zo) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,zo("rpc")_"/"_zo("direction")_"/"_zo("schema_version"),"ORWU GLOBAL/resp/1","header parses rpc, direction and schema_version") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP"),@zr

chunk.synopsis: The i-th RAW payload chunk of a v2 record ("" if absent).
examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") set rec("dir")="resp",rec("payload")="chunkbody",rec("result_kind")="scalar" do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$appendRec^VSLTAP(.rec) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$chunk^VSLTAP($$head^VSLTAP(),1),"chunkbody","the first RAW payload chunk reads back verbatim") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$chunk^VSLTAP(99,1),"","absent chunk -> empty string")

drainTo.synopsis: Post-ship trim: drop retained entries up to and including `seq`, advance tail.

params.name: seq
params.doc: the highest shipped sequence (bounded to head)

returns.doc: the drain self-KILLs shipped entries (spec Â§4.1.3)

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$append^VSLTAP("r1"),zz=$$append^VSLTAP("r2"),zz=$$append^VSLTAP("r3"),zz=$$append^VSLTAP("r4"),zz=$$append^VSLTAP("r5") do drainTo^VSLTAP(3) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$size^VSLTAP(),2,"after draining through seq 3, 2 of 5 remain") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")

kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$append^VSLTAP("a") do drainTo^VSLTAP(99) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$tail^VSLTAP(),$$head^VSLTAP(),"drainTo is bounded to head (never advances past it)") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
drainTo.description:
Called by the SEPARATE flush process (VSLS3 $$drain) AFTER a batch ships
200 â€” never from the RPC path. Idempotent; never advances past head.

disable.synopsis: Auto-failover: disable the tap, record an off-window (explicit, never silent).
examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),disable^VSLTAP("pressure") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$disabled^VSLTAP(),"pressure","disable records the reason") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),disable^VSLTAP("latency") do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$offWindows^VSLTAP(.zo)'<1,"disable opens an off-window (explicit, never silent)") kill ^VSLTAP

disabled.synopsis: The auto-failover reason, or "" if armed/clean.
examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$disabled^VSLTAP(),"","armed/clean -> empty reason") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),disable^VSLTAP("fault") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$disabled^VSLTAP(),"fault","reports the auto-failover reason") kill ^VSLTAP

rearm.synopsis: Re-arm after a clean cool-down (D-4): clear the disable + close the off-window.
examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),disable^VSLTAP("pressure"),rearm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$disabled^VSLTAP(),"","re-arm clears the disable reason") kill ^VSLTAP

kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),disable^VSLTAP("pressure"),rearm^VSLTAP() set zz=$$offWindows^VSLTAP(.zo) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$piece(zo(1),"^",3)'="","re-arm closes the off-window (sets the close stamp)") kill ^VSLTAP

offWindows.synopsis: Populate out(1..N) with the recorded off-windows; return the count.

params.name: out
params.doc: by-ref; killed then filled with open^reason^close rows

examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$offWindows^VSLTAP(.zo),0,"no failover yet -> zero off-windows") kill ^VSLTAP

kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),disable^VSLTAP("pressure") set zz=$$offWindows^VSLTAP(.zo) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$piece(zo(1),"^",2),"pressure","the recorded off-window carries its reason") kill ^VSLTAP

returns.doc: OFF | AUTO-DISABLED | UNHEALTHY | ACTIVE | ARMED-IDLE

examples: kill ^VSLTAP do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$state^VSLTAP(),"OFF","unconfigured -> OFF")
kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),heartbeat^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$state^VSLTAP(),"ACTIVE","armed + healthy + consumer -> ACTIVE") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),heartbeat^VSLTAP(),disable^VSLTAP("latency") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$state^VSLTAP(),"AUTO-DISABLED","failover -> AUTO-DISABLED") kill ^VSLTAP

heartbeat.synopsis: Stamp the liveness heartbeat (the watchdog beats this every N seconds).
