examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),heartbeat^VSLTAP() do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$healthy^VSLTAP()=1,"a fresh heartbeat -> healthy") kill ^VSLTAP

healthy.synopsis: 1 iff the heartbeat is fresh within the staleness bound (k8s-style liveness).

returns.doc: a stale/absent heartbeat -> 0 (UNHEALTHY) even with zero traffic

examples: kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP(),heartbeat^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$healthy^VSLTAP(),1,"fresh heartbeat -> healthy") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP do arm^VSLTAP() set ^VSLTAP("hb")=0 do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$healthy^VSLTAP(),0,"stale heartbeat -> not healthy") kill ^VSLTAP
kill ^VSLTAP do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$healthy^VSLTAP(),0,"absent heartbeat -> not healthy")

purgeNode.synopsis: Write ^XTMP("VSLTAP",0)=purgedate^createdate^description so Kernel XQ82 reaps it.
examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do purgeNode^VSLTAP() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$length($get(^XTMP("VSLTAP",0)),"^"),3,"purge node is purgedate^createdate^description") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")

kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do purgeNode^VSLTAP() do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,+$piece(^XTMP("VSLTAP",0),"^",1)'<+$piece(^XTMP("VSLTAP",0),"^",2),"purgedate >= createdate (FileMan internal dates)") kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP")
purgeNode.description:
The SAC ^XTMP convention (XU/krn_8_0_dg_xtmp_global_ug): a ,0) node of
FileMan internal dates lets `XQ XUTL $J NODES` auto-purge the cache. No
FileMan file (Â§4.1.1). Scheduling the option is an install-time seam.

VSLTAPBO.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” traffic-tap back-out / verify-clean (the G-UNINST gate).
VSLTAPBO.description:
doc: @exrun bare

Phase 5 / M4 (GA), plan stage 5.1. v-pkg's KIDS uninstall is routine-only
(it removes the routines + the #9.7/#9.6 build records); it deliberately
leaves the tap's RUNTIME footprint behind. VSLTAPBO reverses exactly that
footprint and then PROVES, by inspection, that nothing is orphaned â€” the
reversible-install invariant a fleet rollout depends on (risk G-UNINST):

  1. the scheduled flush / fidelity TaskMan jobs (recorded in ^VSLTAP("task"))
  2. the tap's XPAR #8989.51 PARAMETER instances + definitions (the config seam)
  3. the ^XTMP("VSLTAP",â€¦) rolling capture cache (the SAC scratch global)
  4. the ^VSLTAP control state (cfg / disabled / hb / _offwindows / hl / fc)

*** Layer: v (above the m/v waterline). The state-global cleanup (3,4) is
pure M and runs on a BARE engine; the XPAR (2) and TaskMan (1) legs are
VistA seams, each fault-fenced so a bare engine survives them and a partial
VistA absence never blocks the cleanup. Order matters: dequeue tasks (their
numbers live in ^VSLTAP) BEFORE the state kill removes the record.

Public API:
  do backout()              full back-out: dequeue tasks -> drop params -> kill state
  $$verifyClean(detail)     1 iff no tap residue; detail(globals/params/tasks) names any
  do cleanState()           kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP") + ^VSLTAP (the bare-testable core)
  do cleanParams()          drop the tap #8989.51 XPAR instances + definitions (fenced)
  do cleanTasks()           dequeue the recorded flush/fidelity TaskMan jobs (fenced)
  $$params(out)             the canonical tap XPAR param-name list -> count

params.synopsis: Fill out(1..N) with the tap's XPAR #8989.51 param names; return N.

params.name: out
params.doc: by-ref; killed then filled out(1)=name â€¦ out(N)=name

returns.doc: the count of tap params (the KIDS build + the back-out share this list)

examples: do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$params^VSLTAPBO(.out)>0,"params: the tap ships at least one XPAR param")
new out do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$params^VSLTAPBO(.out)_"|"_out(7),"10|VSL S3 ENDPOINT","params: 10 knobs, the 7th is the S3 endpoint")
params.description:
The durable install-time config + the S3 deployment knobs. Operator
RUNTIME state (mode/consumer) stays in ^VSLTAP("cfg"), not XPAR.

backout.synopsis: Full back-out: dequeue tasks, drop the XPAR params, kill the state. Idempotent.
examples: set ^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",1)="rec",^VSLTAP("hb")=$horolog do backout^VSLTAPBO() do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$verifyClean^VSLTAPBO(.detail),"backout: a seeded footprint verifies clean afterward")
backout.description:
Each leg is independently fault-fenced, so a missing TaskMan/XPAR/FileMan
(a bare engine, or a partial install) never blocks the state cleanup. Tasks
first â€” their numbers live in ^VSLTAP, which the state kill then removes.

cleanState.synopsis: Kill the rolling capture cache and ALL VSL control state.
examples: set ^VSLTAP("cfg","mode")="armed",^XTMP("VSLTAP","data",1)="x" do cleanState^VSLTAPBO() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$data(^VSLTAP)+$data(^XTMP("VSLTAP")),0,"cleanState: both the cache and the control state are gone")
cleanState.description:
The whole tap footprint that lives in globals: ^XTMP("VSLTAP",â€¦) (the
SAC auto-purge scratch cache) and ^VSLTAP (cfg/disabled/hb/_offwindows/
hl/fc/task). Pure M â€” no VistA needed; this is what the bare suite proves.

cleanParams.synopsis: Drop every tap XPAR param: clear the SYS instance, delete the #8989.51 definition.
examples: do cleanParams^VSLTAPBO() do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,1,"cleanParams: the fenced XPAR leg returns without raising on a bare engine")
cleanParams.description:
Fault-fenced as a whole (a bare engine has no XPAR/FileMan), and per-param
inside delParam, so one missing param does not strand the rest.

delParam.synopsis: (private) clear the SYS-level instance, then delete the #8989.51 definition record.
examples: do delParam^VSLTAPBO("VSL TAP CAP") do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,1,"delParam: a not-present param is a clean no-op (fenced) on a bare engine")

cleanTasks.synopsis: Dequeue every recorded flush/fidelity TaskMan job (read BEFORE cleanState).
examples: do cleanTasks^VSLTAPBO() do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,1,"cleanTasks: the fenced TaskMan leg returns without raising on a bare engine")
cleanTasks.description:
The scheduled-task numbers live in ^VSLTAP("task",label)=ztsk. The
fidelity/flush jobs are periodic re-queues (NOT persistent listeners),
so they dequeue cleanly. Fenced â€” no TaskMan on a bare engine.

dequeue.synopsis: (private) unschedule task `ztsk` via the Kernel ZTLOAD programmer API. Fenced.

params.name: ztsk
params.doc: the task number to remove from the schedule

examples: do dequeue^VSLTAPBO(0) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,1,"dequeue: a non-positive task number is a clean no-op")

verifyClean.synopsis: 1 iff no tap residue remains across all layers; detail() names any survivor.

params.name: detail
params.doc: OUT by-ref; killed then filled detail(globals/params/tasks)

returns.doc: 1 iff globals, XPAR params and tasks are all clean

examples: kill ^XTMP("VSLTAP"),^VSLTAP do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$verifyClean^VSLTAPBO(.detail),"verifyClean: an empty system verifies clean")
verifyClean.description:
Globals (cache + control state) are checked on any engine; the XPAR-param
and task legs are fenced VistA seams â€” a bare engine reports them clean.

paramsResidue.synopsis: (private) 1 iff any tap #8989.51 definition survives (fenced; bare -> 0).
examples: do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$paramsResidue^VSLTAPBO(.detail),0,"paramsResidue: a bare engine (no FileMan) reports no surviving XPAR definitions")

VSLTAPFC.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” fidelity comparator: byte-equality proof, not assertion.
VSLTAPFC.description:
doc: @exrun bare
m-lint: disable-file=M-MOD-024
M-MOD-024 false positives: `t` is populated by reference by $$parse^STDJSON
(and `res` by reconcile's helpers); the analyser cannot see through the
extrinsic/by-ref call, so it reads them as "read before defined".

Phase 3 / M2 of the RPC+HL7 -> S3 traffic tap (spec Â§7). VSLTAPFC is the
standing, gated, byte-level equivalence check that answers "is the captured
copy full-fidelity correct?" â€” by COMPARISON, never by claim. It decodes a
shipped envelope back to the raw bytes and proves they byte-equal the
captured source (RPC: the in-app tee vs the independent passive mirror; HL7:
the shipped object vs its #772 source), and it reconciles a whole corpus
against the read-back objects â€” every record present exactly once, in
sequence, byte-matched, no unaccounted drop (the Â§15.2 round-trip core).
Fidelity is BYTE-EQUALITY against the source â€” NO digest/hash is used (the tap
only observes plain RPC bytes; it adds no crypto). A mismatch is a FAILURE,
surfaced on the console and red-gated in CI.

*** Layer: v (above the m/v waterline). It consumes `m` DOWN only â€” STDJSON
(parse the LDJSON envelope), STDB64 (decode a base64
payload). It produces no traffic and reaches no engine: pure comparison
over envelopes and source records, so it runs on a bare engine and as a CI
gate over recorded fixtures (the live read-back leg is VSLS3 $$readback,
exercised by the integration harness).

Public API:
  $$payloadOf(line)            decode one envelope line back to the raw bytes
  $$matches(line,source)       1 iff the decoded payload byte-equals source
  $$reconcile(corpus,envs,res) round-trip reconcile; res(matched/mismatch/missing/extra)
  $$drops(envs,res)            FU-15 loss taxonomy: rpc_error/rpc_denied by call_id reconcile
  $$manifest(res,ts)           serialise a fidelity run to a JSON manifest line
  do persist(res,ts)           store the last run at ^VSLTAP("fc","last")
  $$lastFidelity()             the last persisted manifest line, or "" (none yet)

payloadOf.synopsis: Decode one LDJSON envelope line back to the verbatim captured bytes.

params.name: line
params.doc: one VSLS3 schema-v1 envelope line (raw or base64 payload)

returns.doc: the raw payload, byte-exact (escaping/base64 reversed)

examples: new rec,opt,line set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-1",rec("seq")=1,rec("payload")=("a"_$char(9)_"b\c"_""""_"q") set line=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$payloadOf^VSLTAPFC(line),"a"_$char(9)_"b\c"_""""_"q","inline payload decodes byte-exact")

new rec,opt,line set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-1",rec("seq")=1,rec("payload")=("a"_$char(9)_"b"_""""_"q") set opt("base64")=1 set line=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$payloadOf^VSLTAPFC(line),"a"_$char(9)_"b"_""""_"q","base64 payload decodes byte-exact")
write $$payloadOf^VSLTAPFC("not json at all")  ; ""

matches.synopsis: 1 iff the decoded payload byte-equals `source` (the fidelity proof).

params.name: source
params.doc: the captured source record (the tee, the #772 message)

returns.doc: the byte-equality proof (RPC tee-vs-mirror; HL7 vs #772)

examples: new rec,opt,line set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-5",rec("seq")=5,rec("payload")="hello world" set line=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$matches^VSLTAPFC(line,"hello world"),"the decoded payload byte-equals the source")

new rec,opt,line set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-5",rec("seq")=5,rec("payload")="hello world" set line=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$matches^VSLTAPFC(line,"hello WORLD"),0,"any drift from the source fails byte-equality")

reconcile.synopsis: Reconcile a generated corpus against the read-back envelopes, by sequence.

params.name: corpus
params.doc: by-ref: corpus(seq) = the generated verbatim record

params.name: envs
params.doc: by-ref: envs(seq)   = the read-back envelope line

returns.doc:
1 iff EVERY corpus record is present exactly once,
byte-equal, with no missing and no extra

examples: new corpus,envs,res,r1,r2,o1,o2 set corpus(1)="record-1",corpus(2)="record-2" set r1("direction")="resp",r1("call_id")="c1",r1("seq")=1,r1("payload")="record-1" set envs(1)=$$envelope^VSLS3(.r1,.o1) set r2("direction")="resp",r2("call_id")="c2",r2("seq")=2,r2("payload")="record-2" set envs(2)=$$envelope^VSLS3(.r2,.o2) do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$reconcile^VSLTAPFC(.corpus,.envs,.res),"a faithful round-trip reconciles fully")

new corpus,envs,res,r1,o1 set corpus(1)="record-1",corpus(2)="record-2" set r1("direction")="resp",r1("call_id")="c1",r1("seq")=1,r1("payload")="record-1" set envs(1)=$$envelope^VSLS3(.r1,.o1) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$reconcile^VSLTAPFC(.corpus,.envs,.res)_"|"_res("matched")_"|"_res("missing"),"0|1|1","a dropped record (seq 2) is flagged missing, reconcile fails")
reconcile.description:
A drift (payload differs from the source) is a mismatch; a dropped record
is missing; a record shipped under a seq not in the corpus is extra.
Drops are only acceptable if accounted for in _offwindows (out of band).

drops.synopsis: Classify the loss taxonomy by grouping the shipped envelopes on call_id (FU-15).

params.name: envs
params.doc: by-ref: envs(k) = one shipped schema-v1 envelope line (any key)

returns.doc: the total number of accounted drops (rpc_error + rpc_denied)

examples: new envs,res,rq,rs,oq,os,rd,od,re,oe set rq("direction")="req",rq("call_id")="A",rq("seq")=1,rq("denied")=0,rq("payload")="p" set envs(1)=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rq,.oq) set rs("direction")="resp",rs("call_id")="A",rs("seq")=2,rs("payload")="r" set envs(2)=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rs,.os) set rd("direction")="req",rd("call_id")="B",rd("seq")=3,rd("denied")=1,rd("payload")="p" set envs(3)=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rd,.od) set re("direction")="req",re("call_id")="C",re("seq")=4,re("denied")=0,re("payload")="p" set envs(4)=$$envelope^VSLS3(.re,.oe) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$drops^VSLTAPFC(.envs,.res)_"|"_res("rpc_denied")_"|"_res("rpc_error"),"2|1|1","a denied req-no-resp is rpc_denied, an errored req-no-resp is rpc_error, the clean pair is not a drop")
drops.description:
A `call_id` with a req and NO resp is a loss: `rpc_denied` if the req carries
denied=1 (CHKPRMIT short-circuit, never dispatched), else `rpc_error` (a runtime
fault in the dispatch â†’ the broker trap re-dispatches and the resp side-call is
never reached â€” FU-16). Both are EXPECTED, accounted outcomes (high-value for an
analysis tap), recorded in the _drops manifest, never a silent gap (schema-lock Â§5).

manifest.synopsis: Serialise a fidelity run to a single JSON manifest line (the _fidelity object).

returns.doc: one RFC-8259 JSON object summarising the run

examples: new res,t set res("matched")=10,res("mismatch")=0,res("missing")=0,res("extra")=0 do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$parse^STDJSON($$manifest^VSLTAPFC(.res,"65800,43200"),.t)&($$valueOf^STDJSON(t("matched"))=10)&($$type^STDJSON(t("ok"))="true"),"a clean run serialises to well-formed JSON with matched=10 and ok=true")

new res,t set res("matched")=3,res("mismatch")=2,res("missing")=0,res("extra")=0 do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$parse^STDJSON($$manifest^VSLTAPFC(.res,"65800,43200"),.t)_"|"_$$type^STDJSON(t("ok")),"1|false","a run with a mismatch serialises ok=false")
manifest.description:
ok=true iff there were zero mismatches, zero missing and zero extras â€”
the standing byte-equality proof shipped under traffic/<st>/_fidelity/.

persist.synopsis: Store the last fidelity run so the console can read it (no live run on request).

returns.doc: writes the manifest line to ^VSLTAP("fc","last")

examples: new res,t,save,had set had=$data(^VSLTAP("fc","last")),save=$get(^VSLTAP("fc","last")) set res("matched")=8,res("mismatch")=0,res("missing")=0,res("extra")=0 do persist^VSLTAPFC(.res,"65800,43200") do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$parse^STDJSON($$lastFidelity^VSLTAPFC(),.t)_"|"_$$valueOf^STDJSON(t("matched")),"1|8","persist stores a readable manifest carrying the matched count") if had set ^VSLTAP("fc","last")=save
persist.description:
$$verify/$$reconcile/$$manifest compute fidelity ON CALL against a corpus;
VWEBT (the console) needs a passive getter, so the periodic comparator (and
the make test-s3 round-trip) call persist after a run. Single "last" slot:
a newer run replaces the prior one (the console shows the latest result).

lastFidelity.synopsis: The last persisted _fidelity manifest line, or "" when no run has run yet.

returns.doc: the JSON manifest stored by persist, or "" (console: "pending")

examples: new res,save,had set had=$data(^VSLTAP("fc","last")),save=$get(^VSLTAP("fc","last")) set res("matched")=5,res("mismatch")=0,res("missing")=0,res("extra")=0 do persist^VSLTAPFC(.res,"65800,1") do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$lastFidelity^VSLTAPFC()'="","after a persisted run lastFidelity returns the stored manifest line") if had set ^VSLTAP("fc","last")=save
lastFidelity.description: A pure read of the VSL control state â€” the snapshot reader (VWEBT) parses it.
lastFidelity.illustrative: the empty-state contract ("" when no run has run) needs a clean slot with no prior persisted run; shown read-only as a self-restoring round-trip in the example above

VSLTAPHL.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” tap health instrument + standby readiness (the watchdog).
VSLTAPHL.description:
doc: @exrun bare

The always-on instrument behind the tap (spec Â§6.2/Â§6.4/Â§8/Â§8.1): cheap
counters/timers, the A/B latency watchdog that FEEDS auto-failover, and the
standby readiness probe + synthetic canary that prove a gated, idle tap is
healthy â€” NOT dead. On for both regimes (tap on/off); the counters make the
always-on capture tax visible in real time even with no consumer.

Layer: v. Consumes the VSLTAP core (v->v: cfg/disable/heartbeat/healthy). No
egress in Phase 2 â€” the readiness "egress ping" reduces to the substrate +
fence checks; the real S3 HEAD ping is the Phase-3 consumer-presence wiring.

Counters (^VSLTAP("hl",â€¦) â€” VSL namespace):
  writes  captured-record count        bytes  bytes-to-buffer
  denied  consumer-gate denials         s,i    bounded latency-sample window

Public API:
  do record(us,bytes,denied)   record one capture sample (us=0 -> count only, no latency)
  $$writes() / $$bytes() / $$denied()    counter reads
  $$pctl(p)                    p-th percentile capture latency (nearest-rank)
  $$abcheck(base,tapped)       1 iff tapped-base exceeds the D-7 latency bound
  do watchLatency(base,tapped) trip auto-failover OFF on a latency-delta breach
  $$ready()                    standby readiness probe (idle -> provably healthy)
  $$canary()                   synthetic byte-exact round-trip through ^XTMP (no real RPC)
  do beat()                    update the liveness heartbeat

record.synopsis: Record one capture sample: a denial, or a write (+bytes, +optional latency).

params.name: us
params.doc: capture latency in microseconds (0 -> no latency sample)

params.name: denied
params.doc: 1 iff the consumer-gate denied this capture (no write)

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do record^VSLTAPHL(120,50,0),record^VSLTAPHL(80,40,0),record^VSLTAPHL(0,0,1) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$writes^VSLTAPHL(),2,"two capture writes counted (the denied sample is not a write)")
kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do record^VSLTAPHL(120,50,0),record^VSLTAPHL(80,40,0) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$bytes^VSLTAPHL(),90,"bytes-to-buffer summed across writes")
kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do record^VSLTAPHL(0,0,1) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$denied^VSLTAPHL(),1,"one consumer-gate denial counted")

pctl.synopsis: The p-th percentile (nearest-rank) of the latency-sample window; 0 if none.

returns.doc: the nearest-rank sample value

examples: new i kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") for i=1:1:100 do record^VSLTAPHL(i,1,0)  do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,($$pctl^VSLTAPHL(50)'<1)&($$pctl^VSLTAPHL(50)'>100),"p50 of 1..100 falls within [1,100]")
new i kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") for i=1:1:100 do record^VSLTAPHL(i,1,0)  do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$pctl^VSLTAPHL(95)'<$$pctl^VSLTAPHL(50),"p95 >= p50 (percentiles are monotonic)")

abcheck.synopsis: 1 iff (tapped - base) exceeds the pre-registered D-7 latency bound.

returns.doc: the exact signal the Â§6.2 watchdog trips on

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") set ^VSLTAP("cfg","latbound")=100 do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$abcheck^VSLTAPHL(10,60),0,"tapped-base=50 <= 100 bound -> clean")
kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") set ^VSLTAP("cfg","latbound")=100 do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$abcheck^VSLTAPHL(10,510),1,"tapped-base=500 > 100 bound -> trip")

watchLatency.synopsis: Trip auto-failover OFF when the tapped-vs-baseline delta breaches the bound.
examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1) set ^VSLTAP("cfg","latbound")=100 do watchLatency^VSLTAPHL(10,10) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$disabled^VSLTAP(),"","a within-bound sample does not disable the tap")

kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP(),setConsumer^VSLTAP(1) set ^VSLTAP("cfg","latbound")=100 do watchLatency^VSLTAPHL(10,500) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$disabled^VSLTAP(),"latency","an over-bound sample self-disables with reason latency")

ready.synopsis: Standby readiness probe: 1 iff a gated/idle tap COULD capture if a consumer appeared.

returns.doc: checks (1) armed (2) not auto-disabled (3) heartbeat fresh

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP(),beat^VSLTAPHL() do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$ready^VSLTAPHL()=1,"readiness probe green while idle (substrate writable, fence armed, heartbeat fresh)")
kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP(),beat^VSLTAPHL() set ^VSLTAP("hb")=0 do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$ready^VSLTAPHL()=0,"a stale heartbeat flips readiness red even with zero traffic")
ready.description: (4) the ^XTMP capture substrate is writable. Egress HEAD ping is Phase 3.

canary.synopsis: Synthetic byte-exact round-trip of a tagged record through ^XTMP â€” touches no real RPC.

returns.doc: 1 iff the capture substrate round-trips byte-exact on standby

examples: kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP(),beat^VSLTAPHL() do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$canary^VSLTAPHL()=1,"canary proves capture-substrate works on standby (byte-exact round-trip)")

kill ^VSLTAP,^XTMP("VSLTAP") do arm^VSLTAP(),beat^VSLTAPHL() set %=$$canary^VSLTAPHL() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$size^VSLTAP(),0,"the canary leaves the real ring empty (no clinical-traffic perturbation)")
canary.description:
Proves capture works while idle without perturbing the ring (the Â§8.1
synthetic monitor; the Â§15 harness run with a one-record corpus).

VSLTAPRUN.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” the periodic fidelity-run task (closes the console loop).
VSLTAPRUN.description:
doc: @exrun bare

Phase 5 / M4 (GA), plan stage 5.1. persist^VSLTAPFC + $$lastFidelity exist
and the VWEBT console reads ^VSLTAP("fc","last"), but in a live install
NOTHING calls persist â€” only the test suite writes it â€” so the console's
fidelity panel shows `pending` forever. VSLTAPRUN is the schedulable task
that closes that loop: on a cadence it reconciles a shipped-vs-source sample
and calls persist, giving the operator a real, current match %.

*** Layer: v. It consumes the fidelity comparator (VSLTAPFC, v) and the gate
(VSLTAP, v) and binds TaskMan (^%ZTLOAD, the same #10063 seam VSLTASK uses)
to re-queue itself. The persist seam ($$reconcilePersist) is pure M and
bare-proven; the live sampler ($$fidelityNow â€” LIST shipped objects, read
them back, integrity-verify each) is the egress leg that lights the console.

Public API:
  do run()                     the scheduled task body (gate -> sample -> persist -> re-queue)
  $$fidelityNow()              LIST shipped objects -> read back -> integrity-verify -> persist -> matched
  $$reconcilePersist(corpus,envs)  reconcile a known corpus then persist it -> ok (the e2e-harness seam)
  $$cadence()                  the run period in seconds (XPAR VSL TAP FIDELITY CADENCE; default 3600)
  $$schedule()                 queue run^VSLTAPRUN at now+cadence; record the task# -> task#

reconcilePersist.synopsis: Reconcile the corpus vs the read-back envelopes, persist the result, return ok.

params.name: envs
params.doc: by-ref: envs(seq)   = the read-back envelope line

returns.doc: 1 iff the sample reconciles byte-perfect (ok=true persisted)

examples: new corpus,envs,rec,opt,save set save=$get(^VSLTAP("fc","last")) set rec("direction")="resp",rec("call_id")="500-1-1",rec("seq")=1,rec("payload")="hello world",corpus(1)="hello world",envs(1)=$$envelope^VSLS3(.rec,.opt) do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$reconcilePersist^VSLTAPRUN(.corpus,.envs),1,"a byte-perfect 1-record sample reconciles ok=true") set ^VSLTAP("fc","last")=save
reconcilePersist.description:
The one call that writes ^VSLTAP("fc","last") in production â€” the live
sampler and the make-test-s3 round-trip both funnel through here so the
console (VWEBT $$lastFidelity^VSLTAPFC) stops showing `pending`.

cadence.synopsis: The fidelity-run period in seconds: XPAR VSL TAP FIDELITY CADENCE, default 3600.

returns.doc: a positive number of seconds between fidelity runs

examples: write $$cadence^VSLTAPRUN()  ; 3600
cadence.description:
$text-guarded â€” a bare engine has no XPAR, so the default (1h) applies.
@icr 2263 @call $$GET^XPAR @status Supported @custodian XU @source XU/krn_8_0_dg_toolkit_ug#getxpar-return-an-instance-of-a-parameter

schedule.synopsis: Queue run^VSLTAPRUN at now+cadence; record the task# (so back-out can dequeue it); return it.

returns.doc: the queued task number, or 0 when there is no TaskMan (bare/no-queue)

examples: write $$schedule^VSLTAPRUN()  ; 0
schedule.description:
A NON-persistent periodic task (each run re-queues the next) â€” NOT a
PSET self-restarting listener (so it is cleanly dequeueable by VSLTAPBO,
unlike a persistent listener). Records ^VSLTAP("task","fidelity")=task#.
$text-guarded so a bare engine is a clean no-op.
@icr 10063 @call ^%ZTLOAD @status Supported @custodian XU @source XU/krn_8_0_tm#callable-entry-points

run.synopsis: The scheduled task body: gate -> sample+persist -> re-queue. Fenced (never aborts TaskMan).
examples: new save set save=$get(^VSLTAP("fc","last")) do off^VSLTAP() set ^VSLTAP("fc","last")="{""sentinel"":1}" do run^VSLTAPRUN() do eq^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$lastFidelity^VSLTAPFC(),"{""sentinel"":1}","a disabled tap skips the live work and leaves the last result untouched") set ^VSLTAP("fc","last")=save
run.description:
Respects the Phase-2 gate â€” when the tap is OFF / auto-disabled / has no
consumer it skips the live work entirely (no false fidelity result). The
whole body is fault-fenced so a sampling/egress fault self-clears and the
next tick is still re-queued.

fidelityNow.synopsis: Sample recently-shipped objects, confirm each reads back as a well-formed envelope, persist the result -> count.

returns.doc:
the count of shipped objects that read back as well-formed
schema-v1 envelopes; -1 if no egress / nothing sampled

examples: write $$fidelityNow^VSLTAPRUN()  ; -1
fidelityNow.description:
The PRODUCTION readback signal that lights the VWEBT console â€” it needs no
generated corpus and no separate process. It LISTs the per-station shipped
objects under `traffic/<station>/<proto>/` (`$$list^VSLS3`), reads each back,
and checks every line PARSES as a schema-v1 envelope -> matched/mismatch. This
proves the ship->store->readback path returns valid objects (it catches gross
storage / transport / truncation corruption). NO hash is used â€” strong byte-
fidelity is the byte-equality reconcile against the source corpus
($$reconcile^VSLTAPFC, the Â§15.2 round-trip), and at-rest integrity is the
storage layer's (S3's) job. Fenced; persists ^VSLTAP("fc","last"), which VWEBT
reads via `$$lastFidelity^VSLTAPFC`.

nextKey.signature: do nextKey^VSLTAPRUN(k, seen, listing, ctx, bucket, opt, res)
nextKey.synopsis: (private) step to the previous listed subscript; verify its object if it's a real key.
nextKey.description: The caller's FOR bounds the count (quit:seen'<cap), so no cap check here.
nextKey.illustrative: private iterator over a $$list^VSLS3 listing array â€” needs a live S3 ctx + bucket + shipped objects to step; exercised end-to-end via $$fidelityNow.

VSLTASK.synopsis: v-stdlib â€” VistA TaskMan persistent-listener adapter (the process seam).
VSLTASK.description:
doc: @exrun live
doc: @exsafe transactional

Binds the persistent-listener seam to Kernel TaskMan. A long-running VSL/
VWEB socket listener is a TaskMan **persistent task**: $$PSET^%ZTLOAD marks
a queued task persistent, so TaskMan automatically RE-RUNS it when the lock
on ^%ZTSCH("TASK",n) is dropped â€” a self-healing listener (architecture
Â§3.5). VSLTASK is a thin binding over the Supported ^%ZTLOAD programmer API
(ICR #10063), NOT new machinery; portable diagnostics belong in STDLOG (v->m),
never re-implemented here.

Public API:
  $$running^VSLTASK()            â€” 1 iff the TaskMan scheduler is live (=$$TM^%ZTLOAD)
  $$stop^VSLTASK()              â€” 1 iff a stop has been requested (=$$S^%ZTLOAD)
  $$persist^VSLTASK(ztsk)       â€” mark queued task `ztsk` self-restarting (=$$PSET^%ZTLOAD)
  $$schedule^VSLTASK(entry,desc,when) â€” headless-queue a persistent listener -> its task#
  $$lastError^VSLTASK()         â€” last error detail, else ""

*** ERROR CONTRACT â€” loud on a malformed call / a real TaskMan fault ***
A malformed call (no entry / no task#) or a TaskMan queue fault maps to a
clean ,U-VSL-TASK-ARG, / ,U-VSL-TASK-QUEUE, $ECODE, with the detail in
^TMP($job,"vsltask","err") for $$lastError. A normal negative â€” "the
scheduler is not running here" ($$running=0) or "no stop requested"
($$stop=0) â€” is NOT an error (kickoff decision 4). The flag-based $ETRAP
pattern is used (NEVER zgoto â€” a zgoto trap aborts the resident harness 0/0,
the M4 VSLLOG gotcha); OUR trap is cleared before any re-raise.

Self-restart note: the restart CONTRACT is bound here ($$PSET^%ZTLOAD marks
^%ZTSCH("TASK",n,"P"); TaskMan re-runs on a lock drop). Observing a live
restart needs the task body installed as a RESIDENT routine (the VSLBLD/
v-pkg path) + lock manipulation, and a persistent task is deliberately
un-KILLable â€” out of scope for a safe unit test (see VSLTASKTST).

running.synopsis: 1 iff the TaskMan scheduler is live (its ^%ZTSCH("RUN") heartbeat is fresh).

returns.doc: 1 iff TaskMan is running (the self-heal precondition); 0 otherwise

examples: do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,($$running^VSLTASK()=0)!($$running^VSLTASK()=1),"$$running returns a clean boolean (resolves $$TM^%ZTLOAD)")

stop.synopsis: 1 iff a stop has been requested of the currently-running task (cooperative stop).

returns.doc: 1 iff the listener loop should stop; 0 when not in a task / no stop pending

examples: do true^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$stop^VSLTASK()=0,"$$stop=0 when not running as a TaskMan task (the cooperative-stop check)")

persist.synopsis: Mark queued task `ztsk` persistent so TaskMan self-restarts it on a lock drop.

returns.doc: 1 iff the task was marked persistent, else 0 (task not queued)

raises.doc: the call is malformed (no positive task number)

examples: do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set x=$$persist^VSLTASK("""")","U-VSL-TASK-ARG","$$persist with no task# raises U-VSL-TASK-ARG")
persist.illustrative: the success path marks a queued task persistent (un-KILLable) on a shared live TaskMan; only the malformed-call contract is safely shown

schedule.synopsis: Headless-queue a persistent listener at `entry`; return its task number.

params.name: when
params.doc: $H start time (optional; default now). MUST be <=5-digit $H or "@"

returns.doc: the queued task number

examples: do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set x=$$schedule^VSLTASK("""",""ZZ"")","U-VSL-TASK-ARG","$$schedule with no entry raises U-VSL-TASK-ARG")
schedule.illustrative: the success path queues a real, persistent (un-KILLable) TaskMan task on a shared live VistA â€” a side effect that cannot be cleanly undone; only the malformed-call contract is safely shown

raises_nodemo.reason: reachable only via a genuinely-failed live TaskMan queue (side-effecting, non-deterministic); not safely triggerable in an example

lastError.synopsis: The last VSLTASK error message (the composed malformed-call / fault detail).

returns.doc: ^TMP($job,"vsltask","err"), or "" if none

examples: do raises^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,"set x=$$persist^VSLTASK("""")","U-VSL-TASK-ARG","arm an error") do contains^STDASSERT(.pass,.fail,$$lastError^VSLTASK(),"task number","$$lastError carries the malformed-call detail after a raise")

queue.synopsis: (private) headless ^%ZTLOAD queue (no device); return the task number, else 0.
queue.illustrative: private; runs ^%ZTLOAD which queues a real TaskMan task on a shared live VistA â€” a side effect that cannot be cleanly undone in a unit test
